## 4) `src/pages/Dashboard.jsx`
```jsx
// src/pages/Dashboard.jsx
import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext';
import { getProjects } from '../utils/api';
import AddProjectForm from './AddProjectForm';

export default function Dashboard() {
  const { user } = useAuth();
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
  const [err, setErr] = useState('');

  const load = async () => {
    setLoading(true);
    setErr('');
    const data = await getProjects();
    const list = Array.isArray(data) ? data : data.projects || [];
    setProjects(list);
    setLoading(false);
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    load();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-gray-50 p-6">
      <div className="max-w-3xl mx-auto space-y-6">
        <header className="flex items-center justify-between">
          <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold">
            Welcome{user?.name ? `, ${user.name}` : user?.email ? `, ${user.email}` : ''}
          </h1>
        </header>

        <section className="bg-white rounded shadow p-4">
          <h2 className="text-lg font-semibold mb-3">Create Project</h2>
          <AddProjectForm onCreated={load} />
        </section>

        <section className="bg-white rounded shadow p-4">
          <h2 className="text-lg font-semibold mb-3">Your Projects</h2>
          {loading && <p className="text-gray-500">Loading...</p>}
          {err && <p className="text-red-600">{err}</p>}
          {!loading && !err && (
            <ul className="divide-y">
              {projects.map((p) => (
                <li key={p._id} className="py-2">{p.name || p.title}</li>
              ))}
              {projects.length === 0 && (
                <li className="py-2 text-gray-500">No projects yet.</li>
              )}
            </ul>
          )}
        </section>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

```jsx
// src/pages/AddProjectForm.jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { createProject } from '../utils/api';

export default function AddProjectForm({ onCreated }) {
  const [name, setName] = useState('');
  const [busy, setBusy] = useState(false);
  const [msg, setMsg] = useState('');

  const submit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    if (!name.trim()) return;
    setBusy(true);
    setMsg('Creating...');
    await createProject({ name });
    setName('');
    setMsg('✅ Created');
    if (onCreated) onCreated();
    setBusy(false);
  };

  return (
    <form onSubmit={submit} className="space-y-3">
      <input
        value={name}
        onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)}
        placeholder="Project name"
        className="w-full border border-gray-300 rounded px-3 py-2"
      />
      <button
        type="submit"
        disabled={busy}
        className="bg-green-600 text-white rounded px-4 py-2 hover:bg-green-700 disabled:opacity-60"
      >
        {busy ? 'Creating...' : 'Add Project'}
      </button>
      <p className="text-sm text-gray-600">{msg}</p>
    </form>
  );
}
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the updated `AuthContext.js` with safe `localStorage` parsing, fallback defaults, and adjusted `useAuth` hook:  

```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.js
import React, { createContext, useState, useContext, useEffect } from 'react';

const AuthContext = createContext(null);

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const safeParse = (key, fallback) => {
    try {
      const stored = localStorage.getItem(key);
      return stored ? JSON.parse(stored) : fallback;
    } catch {
      return fallback;
    }
  };

  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse('user', null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => safeParse('token', null));

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem('user');
    }
  }, [user]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem('token', JSON.stringify(token));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem('token');
    }
  }, [token]);

  const login = (userData, tokenValue) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(tokenValue);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => {
  const context = useContext(AuthContext);
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error('useAuth must be used within an AuthProvider');
  }
  return context;
};
```

This ensures:  
- JSON parsing errors from `localStorage` won't crash the app.  
- Defaults (`null`) are used if parsing fails or key is missing.  
- `useAuth` safely enforces usage inside `AuthProvider`.  

If you want, I can now **bundle this with the API utility refactor** you’ve been requesting so the login and dashboard styles work consistently. Would you like me to prepare that full set in one go?

USER:
Refactor the API helper module to export named functions for login, getProjects, createProject, and default axios instance with baseURL and token interceptor.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the refactored **utils/api.js** with named exports and an axios instance that includes a token interceptor:  

```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from 'axios';

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
});

// Attach token to every request if available
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const storedUser = localStorage.getItem('user');
  let token = null;

  try {
    token = storedUser ? JSON.parse(storedUser).token : null;
  } catch {
    token = null;
  }

  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named API functions
export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const { data } = await api.post('/api/users/login', credentials);
  return data;
};

export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const { data } = await api.post('/api/users/register', userData);
  return data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const { data } = await api.get('/api/projects');
  return data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const { data } = await api.post('/api/projects', projectData);
  return data;
};

// Export default axios instance for optional direct use
export default api;
```

This will:  
- Set `baseURL` to `http://localhost:5000`.  
- Automatically attach the stored token from `localStorage` to all API requests.  
- Export **named functions** (`loginUser`, `registerUser`, `getProjects`, `createProject`) for direct component imports.  
- Maintain `api` as the default export if needed for custom calls.  

If you want, I can now adjust **Login.jsx**, **Dashboard.jsx**, and **AddProjectForm.jsx** to match these named exports so your app runs without endpoint or token issues.  
Do you want me to do that next?

### **`src/utils/api.js`**
```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from 'axios';

// Create axios instance with baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000', // no trailing /api
});

// Interceptor to add Authorization header if token exists
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named exports for API functions
export const registerUser = (userData) => api.post('/api/auth/register', userData);
export const loginUser = (credentials) => api.post('/api/auth/login', credentials);
export const getProjects = () => api.get('/api/projects');
export const createProject = (projectData) => api.post('/api/projects', projectData);

export default api;
```

---

### **`src/pages/Login.jsx`**
```javascript
// src/pages/Login.jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom';
import { loginUser } from '../utils/api';
import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext';

const Login = () => {
  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ email: '', password: '' });
  const [error, setError] = useState('');
  const navigate = useNavigate();
  const { setAuthData } = useAuth();

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    setFormData({ ...formData, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const { data } = await loginUser(formData);
      localStorage.setItem('token', data.token);
      localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(data.user));
      setAuthData({ token: data.token, user: data.user });
      navigate('/dashboard');
    } catch (err) {
      setError('Invalid credentials');
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="login-container">
      <h2>Login</h2>
      {error && <p className="error">{error}</p>}
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <input
          type="email"
          name="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          value={formData.email}
          onChange={handleChange}
          required
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          name="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          value={formData.password}
          onChange={handleChange}
          required
        />
        <button type="submit">Log In</button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

---

### **`src/pages/Dashboard.jsx`**
```javascript
// src/pages/Dashboard.jsx
import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import { getProjects } from '../utils/api';
import AddProjectForm from './AddProjectForm';

const Dashboard = () => {
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const fetchProjects = async () => {
      try {
        const { data } = await getProjects();
        setProjects(data);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error('Error fetching projects', err);
      }
    };
    fetchProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="dashboard-container">
      <h2>Dashboard</h2>
      <AddProjectForm onProjectAdded={setProjects} />
      <ul>
        {projects.map((project) => (
          <li key={project._id}>{project.name}</li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Dashboard;
```

---

### **`src/pages/AddProjectForm.jsx`**
```javascript
// src/pages/AddProjectForm.jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { createProject } from '../utils/api';

const AddProjectForm = ({ onProjectAdded }) => {
  const [projectName, setProjectName] = useState('');

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const { data } = await createProject({ name: projectName });
      setProjectName('');
      onProjectAdded((prev) => [...prev, data]);
    } catch (err) {
      console.error('Error creating project', err);
    }
  };

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="add-project-form">
      <input
        type="text"
        placeholder="Project Name"
        value={projectName}
        onChange={(e) => setProjectName(e.target.value)}
        required
      />
      <button type="submit">Add Project</button>
    </form>
  );
};

export default AddProjectForm;
```

```javascript
});

api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

  const response = await api.post('/api/auth/register', userData);
  return response.data;
};

// Named export: Login a user
export const loginUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/auth/login', userData);
  return response.data;
};

// Named export: Get all projects
export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get('/api/projects');
  return response.data;
};

// Named export: Create a new project
export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/projects', projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export the axios instance for direct use if needed
export { api };
```

Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the updated **AuthContext** with safe JSON parsing from `localStorage`, fallback defaults, and a consistent `useAuth` hook:  

```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.jsx
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from 'react';

const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const safeParse = (key, defaultValue) => {
    try {
      const stored = localStorage.getItem(key);
      return stored ? JSON.parse(stored) : defaultValue;
    } catch {
      return defaultValue;
    }
  };

  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse('user', null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => safeParse('token', null));

  useEffect(() => {
    localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(user));
  }, [user]);

  useEffect(() => {
    localStorage.setItem('token', JSON.stringify(token));
  }, [token]);

  const login = (userData, tokenValue) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(tokenValue);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
    localStorage.removeItem('user');
    localStorage.removeItem('token');
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => {
  return useContext(AuthContext);
};
```

This version ensures:
- `localStorage` values are parsed safely with `try/catch`
- Defaults are applied if parsing fails or no value exists
- `useAuth` hook is stable and consistent
- State updates sync back to `localStorage` automatically

If you want, I can now also **refactor your `api.js`** to integrate the token from this updated context for all requests. That would make sure the `Authorization` header is always correct.

USER:
The user needs fixes to AuthContext exports and imports, API utility exports, include Authorization headers in API calls, and restore consistent styles for Login and Dashboard according to prior design.

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the updated implementation covering all points in your request:

---

### **`src/context/AuthContext.jsx`**
```jsx
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from 'react';

const AuthContext = createContext(null);

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => {
    try {
      return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('user')) || null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  });

  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => {
    try {
      return localStorage.getItem('token') || '';
    } catch {
      return '';
    }
  });

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(user));
    else localStorage.removeItem('user');
  }, [user]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (token) localStorage.setItem('token', token);
    else localStorage.removeItem('token');
  }, [token]);

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, setUser, token, setToken }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => useContext(AuthContext);
```

---

### **`src/utils/api.js`**
```javascript
import axios from 'axios';

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000', // No duplicate `/api` prefix
});

// Interceptor to attach token
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  if (token) config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  return config;
});

// Auth endpoints
export const registerUser = (data) => api.post('/api/auth/register', data);
export const loginUser = (data) => api.post('/api/auth/login', data);

// Project endpoints
export const getProjects = () => api.get('/api/projects');
```

```jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext';
import { loginUser } from '../utils/api';
import './Login.css'; // Keep prior consistent design

const Login = () => {
  const { setUser, setToken } = useAuth();
  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ email: '', password: '' });

  const handleChange = (e) =>
    setFormData({ ...formData, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const res = await loginUser(formData);
      setUser(res.data.user);
      setToken(res.data.token);
    } catch (err) {
      console.error('Login failed', err);
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="login-container">
      <form className="login-form" onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <h2>Login</h2>
        <input
          type="email"
          name="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          onChange={handleChange}
          value={formData.email}
          required
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          name="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          onChange={handleChange}
          value={formData.password}
          required
        />
        <button type="submit">Login</button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from 'axios';

// Create Axios instance
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000',
});

// Attach token to every request if available
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Auth
export const login = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post('/auth/login', credentials);
  return response.data;
};

export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/auth/register', userData);
  return response.data;
};

// Projects
export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get('/projects');
  return response.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/projects', projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Default export for flexibility if needed
```

- Removed duplicate `/api` prefix — endpoints are now relative to `http://localhost:5000`.
- Added `Authorization` header interceptor pulling token from `localStorage`.

ASSISTANT:

### **`src/utils/api.js`**
```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from 'axios';

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000', // No /api prefix here
});

// Attach token to all requests if available
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named exports for API functions
export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const res = await api.post('/register', userData);
  return res.data;
};

export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post('/login', credentials);
  return res.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get('/projects');
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const res = await api.post('/projects', projectData);
  return res.data;
};

export default api;
```

---

### **`src/pages/Login.jsx`**
```javascript
// src/pages/Login.jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom';
import { loginUser } from '../utils/api';
import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext';

export default function Login() {
  const { setAuthData } = useAuth();
  const navigate = useNavigate();

  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ email: '', password: '' });
  const [error, setError] = useState('');

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    setFormData({ ...formData, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const { token, user } = await loginUser(formData);
      localStorage.setItem('token', token);
      localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(user));
      setAuthData({ token, user });
      navigate('/dashboard');
    } catch (err) {
      setError('Login failed. Please check your credentials.');
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="auth-container">
      <h2>Login</h2>
      {error && <p className="error-text">{error}</p>}
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <input type="email" name="email" placeholder="Email" value={formData.email} onChange={handleChange} required />
        <input type="password" name="password" placeholder="Password" value={formData.password} onChange={handleChange} required />
        <button type="submit">Login</button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
}
```

### **`src/pages/Dashboard.jsx`**
```javascript
// src/pages/Dashboard.jsx
import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import { getProjects } from '../utils/api';
import AddProjectForm from './AddProjectForm';

export default function Dashboard() {
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

  const fetchProjects = async () => {
    try {
      const data = await getProjects();
      setProjects(data);
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error fetching projects:', error);
    }
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="dashboard-container">
      <h1>Dashboard</h1>
      <AddProjectForm onProjectAdded={fetchProjects} />
      <ul>
        {projects.map((proj) => (
          <li key={proj._id}>{proj.name}</li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </div>
  );
}
```

### **`src/pages/AddProjectForm.jsx`**
```javascript
// src/pages/AddProjectForm.jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { createProject } from '../utils/api';

export default function AddProjectForm({ onProjectAdded }) {
  const [name, setName] = useState('');

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    if (!name.trim()) return;

    try {
      await createProject({ name });
      setName('');
      onProjectAdded();
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error creating project:', error);
    }
  };

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
      <input type="text" placeholder="Project Name" value={name} onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)} required />
      <button type="submit">Add Project</button>
    </form>
  );
}
```

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the refactored **utils/api.js** to match your requirements with named exports and a default axios instance that includes the token interceptor:  

```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from 'axios';

// Create default axios instance
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000', // Adjust if using a different port or proxy
  headers: {
    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  },
});

// Attach Authorization token interceptor
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named API functions
export const login = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/auth/login', credentials);
  return response.data;
};

export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/auth/register', userData);
  return response.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get('/api/projects');
  return response.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/projects', projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Default export for axios instance
export default api;
```

**Key Points in the Refactor:**
- **Named exports** for `login`, `registerUser`, `getProjects`, and `createProject`.
- **Default export** is the configured `api` instance.
- **Interceptor** attaches `Authorization` header automatically if token exists in `localStorage`.
- Base URL is set to `http://localhost:5000` without a duplicate `/api` prefix in components.

USER:
Please update the frontend api.js to export registerUser, loginUser, getProjects, createProject functions matching the component imports.

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the updated `frontend/src/utils/api.js` with **named exports** for `registerUser`, `loginUser`, `getProjects`, and `createProject`, matching component imports and including the token interceptor:  

```javascript
// frontend/src/utils/api.js
import axios from 'axios';

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000', // No duplicate /api
});

// Attach Authorization header if token exists
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const storedAuth = localStorage.getItem('auth');
  if (storedAuth) {
    try {
      const { token } = JSON.parse(storedAuth);
      if (token) {
        config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
      }
    } catch {
      // Ignore JSON parse errors
    }
  }
  return config;
});

// API functions
export const registerUser = (userData) => api.post('/auth/register', userData);
export const loginUser = (credentials) => api.post('/auth/login', credentials);
export const getProjects = () => api.get('/projects');
export const createProject = (projectData) => api.post('/projects', projectData);

export default api; // Optional: keep instance if needed elsewhere
```

This aligns with:
- **Component imports** in `Login.jsx`, `Dashboard.jsx`, `AddProjectForm.jsx`
- No duplicate `/api` in URLs  
- Automatic token injection via **interceptor**

### **`src/utils/api.js`**
```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from 'axios';

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000',
});

// Attach token automatically
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Auth
export const registerUser = (data) => api.post('/register', data);
export const loginUser = (data) => api.post('/login', data);

// Projects
export const getProjects = () => api.get('/projects');
export const createProject = (data) => api.post('/projects', data);
```

### **`src/pages/Login.jsx`**
```javascript
// src/pages/Login.jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext';
import { loginUser } from '../utils/api';
import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom';

export default function Login() {
  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ email: '', password: '' });
  const [error, setError] = useState('');
  const { setUser, setToken } = useAuth();
  const navigate = useNavigate();

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    setFormData({ ...formData, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const res = await loginUser(formData);
      setToken(res.data.token);
      setUser(res.data.user);
      localStorage.setItem('token', res.data.token);
      localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(res.data.user));
      navigate('/dashboard');
    } catch (err) {
      setError(err.response?.data?.message || 'Login failed');
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="auth-container">
      <h2>Login</h2>
      {error && <p className="error">{error}</p>}
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <input
          type="email"
          name="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          value={formData.email}
          onChange={handleChange}
          required
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          name="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          value={formData.password}
          onChange={handleChange}
          required
        />
        <button type="submit">Login</button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
}
```

```javascript
  return (
```

```javascript
        value={name}

        required
      />
```

USER:
Refactor the API utility module to use named exports for login, registerUser, getProjects, createProject, attach auth token via interceptor, and remove default export.

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the refactored **`src/utils/api.js`** with named exports, an Axios instance configured for `http://localhost:5000`, and an interceptor to attach the `Authorization` token automatically:  

```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from 'axios';

// Create Axios instance with baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000',
  headers: {
    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  },
});

// Interceptor to attach token from localStorage
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
}, (error) => Promise.reject(error));

// Named exports for API calls
export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/register', userData);
  return response.data;
};

export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post('/login', credentials);
  return response.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get('/projects');
  return response.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/projects', projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export axios instance for advanced use if needed
export { api };
```

**Key changes made:**
- Removed default export.
- Added `api` Axios instance with `baseURL` set to `http://localhost:5000`.
- Configured `Authorization` header via request interceptor.
- All API functions (`registerUser`, `loginUser`, `getProjects`, `createProject`) are **named exports** to match component imports.

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the refactored **AuthContext** that safely parses values from `localStorage` with fallback defaults and keeps `useAuth` working correctly:  

```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.jsx
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from 'react';

const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  // Safe parse helper
  const safeParse = (key, defaultValue) => {
    try {
      const value = localStorage.getItem(key);
      return value ? JSON.parse(value) : defaultValue;
    } catch {
      return defaultValue;
    }
  };

  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse('user', null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => safeParse('token', null));

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem('user');
    }
  }, [user]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem('token', JSON.stringify(token));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem('token');
    }
  }, [token]);

  const login = (userData, tokenData) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(tokenData);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
    localStorage.removeItem('user');
    localStorage.removeItem('token');
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => {
  return useContext(AuthContext);
};
```

This version:
- Uses a **`safeParse`** function to prevent JSON parsing errors.
- Falls back to `null` (or a provided default) if the stored value is invalid.
- Keeps `useAuth` hook usage the same for components.
- Synchronizes `localStorage` on state change without breaking existing logic.

If you want, I can now **zip this file along with the fixed API utility module** so the AuthContext + API layer are aligned.

---

### **1. `src/context/AuthContext.jsx`**
- Safe localStorage parsing with fallback defaults  
- Clean exports/imports for context and `useAuth` hook  

```jsx
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from 'react';

const AuthContext = createContext(null);

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const storedUser = (() => {
    try {
      return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('user')) || null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  })();

  const storedToken = (() => {
    try {
      return localStorage.getItem('token') || '';
    } catch {
      return '';
    }
  })();

  const [user, setUser] = useState(storedUser);
  const [token, setToken] = useState(storedToken);

  useEffect(() => {
    localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(user));
    localStorage.setItem('token', token);
  }, [user, token]);

  const login = (userData, tokenValue) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(tokenValue);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken('');
    localStorage.removeItem('user');
    localStorage.removeItem('token');
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => useContext(AuthContext);
```

```jsx
import { loginUser } from '../utils/api';
import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext';
import './auth.css'; // restore prior consistent styling

const Login = () => {
  const [email, setEmail] = useState('');
  const [password, setPassword] = useState('');
  const [error, setError] = useState('');
  const navigate = useNavigate();
  const { login } = useAuth();

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const res = await loginUser({ email, password });
      login(res.data.user, res.data.token);
      navigate('/dashboard');
    } catch (err) {
      setError(err.response?.data?.message || 'Login failed');
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="auth-container">
      <form className="auth-form" onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <h2>Login</h2>
        {error && <p className="error">{error}</p>}
        <input
          type="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          value={email}
          onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
          required
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          value={password}
          onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
          required
        />
        <button type="submit">Login</button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

### **`src/utils/api.js`**
```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from 'axios';

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000', // no /api prefix here
});

// Attach token to every request if available
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named exports for API calls
export const registerUser = (userData) => api.post('/api/auth/register', userData);
export const loginUser = (userData) => api.post('/api/auth/login', userData);
export const getProjects = () => api.get('/api/projects');
export const createProject = (projectData) => api.post('/api/projects', projectData);

export default api;
```

### **`src/pages/Login.jsx`**
```javascript
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom';
import { loginUser } from '../utils/api';
import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext';

const Login = () => {
  const [email, setEmail] = useState('');
  const [password, setPassword] = useState('');
  const { setUser, setToken } = useAuth();
  const navigate = useNavigate();

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const res = await loginUser({ email, password });
      localStorage.setItem('token', res.data.token);
      localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(res.data.user));
      setUser(res.data.user);
      setToken(res.data.token);
      navigate('/dashboard');
    } catch (err) {
      console.error('Login failed:', err.response?.data || err.message);
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="login-page">
      <h2>Login</h2>
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <input
          type="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          value={email}
          onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          value={password}
          onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
        />
        <button type="submit">Login</button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

```javascript
        const res = await getProjects();
        setProjects(res.data);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error('Failed to fetch projects:', err.response?.data || err.message);
      }
    };
    fetchProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="dashboard-page">
      <h2>Dashboard</h2>
      <AddProjectForm onProjectAdded={(project) => setProjects([...projects, project])} />
      <ul>
        {projects.map((p) => (
          <li key={p._id}>{p.name}</li>
```

```javascript
      />
```

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the refactored **AuthContext.js** with safe JSON parsing, fallback defaults, and an updated `useAuth` hook:  

**`src/context/AuthContext.js`**  
```javascript
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from 'react';

// Helper to safely parse JSON from localStorage
const safeJSONParse = (key, fallback) => {
  try {
    const storedValue = localStorage.getItem(key);
    return storedValue ? JSON.parse(storedValue) : fallback;
  } catch {
    return fallback;
  }
};

const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeJSONParse('user', null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => localStorage.getItem('token') || '');

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem('user');
    }
  }, [user]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem('token', token);
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem('token');
    }
  }, [token]);

  const login = (userData, tokenValue) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(tokenValue);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken('');
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => {
  const context = useContext(AuthContext);
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error('useAuth must be used within an AuthProvider');
  }
  return context;
};
```

This ensures:  
- All localStorage reads are safely parsed without breaking if malformed JSON is stored.  
- Defaults (`null` for user, `""` for token) are applied if nothing is stored.  
- `useAuth` hook throws an error if used outside of `AuthProvider` (prevents undefined context issues).  

If you want, I can now update **`utils/api.js`** to ensure the token from `AuthContext` is automatically attached to all requests. That will complete the integration.

USER:
Refactor the API utility module to use named exports for login, registerUser, getProjects, createProject, attach auth token via interceptor, and remove default export.

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the refactored `utils/api.js` file with **named exports**, a **token interceptor**, and **no default export**:  

```javascript
// utils/api.js
import axios from 'axios';

// Create Axios instance with baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000', // Adjust if backend URL changes
});

// Interceptor to attach token automatically
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named export for registering a new user
export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/users/register', userData);
  return response.data;
};

// Named export for logging in
export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/users/login', credentials);
  return response.data;
};

// Named export for fetching projects
export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get('/api/projects');
  return response.data;
};

// Named export for creating a new project
export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/projects', projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export the Axios instance for optional use
export { api };
```

**Key changes made**:
- `api` instance configured with `baseURL` pointing to backend root.
- Removed `default export`.
- Added **token interceptor** to automatically include `Authorization` header.
- Exported each API method (`registerUser`, `loginUser`, `getProjects`, `createProject`) individually for better tree-shaking and import clarity.
- Kept `api` instance exported for direct advanced usage.

---

**`src/context/AuthContext.jsx`**
```jsx
import React, { createContext, useState, useContext, useEffect } from 'react';

const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => {
    try {
      return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('user')) || null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  });
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => localStorage.getItem('token') || '');

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem('user');
    }
    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem('token', token);
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem('token');
    }
  }, [user, token]);

  const login = (userData, userToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(userToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken('');
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => useContext(AuthContext);
```

```jsx
import './Login.css'; // Make sure prior consistent styles are restored

const Login = () => {
  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ email: '', password: '' });
  const [error, setError] = useState('');
  const navigate = useNavigate();
  const { login } = useAuth();

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    setFormData({ ...formData, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const { data } = await loginUser(formData);
      login(data.user, data.token);
      navigate('/dashboard');
    } catch (err) {
      setError('Invalid credentials. Please try again.');
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="login-container">
      <form className="login-form" onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <h2>Login</h2>
        {error && <p className="error">{error}</p>}

          type="email"
          name="email"
          placeholder="Email"

          value={formData.email}
          required
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          name="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          onChange={handleChange}
          value={formData.password}
          required
        />
        <button type="submit">Sign In</button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};
```

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the updated **AuthContext.jsx** with safe JSON parsing from `localStorage` and fallback defaults, plus an adjusted `useAuth` hook.  

```jsx
// src/context/AuthContext.jsx
import React, { createContext, useState, useEffect, useContext } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  // Safe parse function
  const safeParse = (key, defaultValue) => {
    try {
      const storedValue = localStorage.getItem(key);
      return storedValue ? JSON.parse(storedValue) : defaultValue;
    } catch {
      return defaultValue;
    }
  };

  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse("user", null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => localStorage.getItem("token") || "");

  // Sync state with localStorage
  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("user");
    }
  }, [user]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem("token", token);
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("token");
    }
  }, [token]);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken("");
    localStorage.removeItem("user");
    localStorage.removeItem("token");
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

// Updated useAuth hook
export const useAuth = () => useContext(AuthContext);
```

This version ensures:  
- **Safe parsing** with `try/catch` to avoid crashes on malformed JSON in `localStorage`.  
- **Fallback defaults** when no value exists.  
- **State syncing** between React state and `localStorage`.  

If you want, I can now also **integrate this with your API calls** so that the `Authorization` header always uses the `token` from `AuthContext`. That way your login and dashboard flows will be consistent.  

Do you want me to proceed with that integration?

### **utils/api.js**

```javascript
// utils/api.js
import axios from 'axios';

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000', // no /api prefix
});

// Attach token to every request
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named exports for each API function

  const res = await api.post('/api/users/register', userData);
  return res.data;
};

  const res = await api.post('/api/users/login', credentials);
  return res.data;
};

  const res = await api.get('/api/projects');
  return res.data;
```

### **Login.jsx**
```javascript
// components/Login.jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { loginUser } from '../utils/api';
import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext';

export default function Login() {
  const { setAuthData } = useAuth();
  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ email: '', password: '' });

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    setFormData({ ...formData, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await loginUser(formData);
      localStorage.setItem('token', data.token);
      localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(data.user));
      setAuthData(data.user, data.token);
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Login failed', error);
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="login-container">
      <h2>Login</h2>
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <input
          type="email"
          name="email"
          value={formData.email}
          onChange={handleChange}
          placeholder="Email"
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          name="password"
          value={formData.password}
          onChange={handleChange}
          placeholder="Password"
        />
        <button type="submit">Login</button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
}
```

```javascript
// components/Dashboard.jsx
import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import { getProjects } from '../utils/api';
import AddProjectForm from './AddProjectForm';

export default function Dashboard() {
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const fetchData = async () => {
      try {
        const data = await getProjects();
        setProjects(data);
      } catch (error) {
        console.error('Error fetching projects', error);
      }
    };
    fetchData();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="dashboard">
      <h2>Dashboard</h2>
      <AddProjectForm onProjectAdded={setProjects} />
      <ul>
        {projects.map((p) => (
          <li key={p._id}>{p.name}</li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </div>
  );
}
```

```javascript
// components/AddProjectForm.jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { createProject, getProjects } from '../utils/api';

export default function AddProjectForm({ onProjectAdded }) {
  const [name, setName] = useState('');

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      await createProject({ name });
      const updated = await getProjects();
      onProjectAdded(updated);
      setName('');
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error creating project', error);
    }
  };

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
      <input
        type="text"
        value={name}
        onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)}
        placeholder="Project Name"
      />
      <button type="submit">Add Project</button>
    </form>
  );
}
```

## **`src/context/AuthContext.jsx`**
```jsx
import { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from 'react';

const AuthContext = createContext(null);

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => {
    try {
      return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('user')) || null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  });

  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => {
    try {
      return localStorage.getItem('token') || '';
    } catch {
      return '';
    }
  });

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(user));
    else localStorage.removeItem('user');

    if (token) localStorage.setItem('token', token);
    else localStorage.removeItem('token');
  }, [user, token]);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken('');
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => useContext(AuthContext);
```

```javascript
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000', // unified base URL
});

// Attach token interceptor
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  if (token) config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  return config;
});

// Named exports for API calls
export const registerUser = (data) => api.post('/api/users/register', data);
export const loginUser = (data) => api.post('/api/users/login', data);
export const getProjects = () => api.get('/api/projects');
export const createProject = (data) => api.post('/api/projects', data);

export default api;
```

## **`src/pages/Login.jsx`**
*(Restored prior styling and updated imports)*  
```jsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext';
import { loginUser } from '../utils/api';
import './Login.css'; // prior consistent styles

export default function Login() {
  const { login } = useAuth();
  const [form, setForm] = useState({ email: '', password: '' });
  const [error, setError] = useState('');

  const handleChange = (e) => setForm({ ...form, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const { data } = await loginUser(form);
      login(data.user, data.token);
    } catch {
      setError('Invalid email or password');
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="login-container">
      <form className="login-form" onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <h2>Sign In</h2>
        {error && <p className="error">{error}</p>}
        <input name="email" type="email" placeholder="Email" value={form.email} onChange={handleChange} required />
        <input name="password" type="password" placeholder="Password" value={form.password} onChange={handleChange} required />
        <button type="submit">Login</button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
}
```

```jsx
import { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext';
import { getProjects } from '../utils/api';
import AddProjectForm from '../components/AddProjectForm';
import './Dashboard.css';

export default function Dashboard() {
  const { user } = useAuth();
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

  useEffect(() => {
    (async () => {
      const { data } = await getProjects();
      setProjects(data);
    })();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="dashboard-container">
      <h1>Welcome, {user?.name}</h1>
      <AddProjectForm onProjectAdded={(project) => setProjects([...projects, project])} />
      <ul className="project-list">
        {projects.map((p) => (
          <li key={p._id}>{p.name}</li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </div>
  );
}
```

```jsx
export default function AddProjectForm({ onProjectAdded }) {

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    if (!name.trim()) return;

    const { data } = await createProject({ name });
    onProjectAdded(data);
    setName('');
  };

  return (
    <form className="add-project-form" onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
      <input value={name} onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)} placeholder="Project name" required />
      <button type="submit">Add Project</button>
    </form>
  );
}
```

```javascript
// frontend/src/utils/api.js
import axios from 'axios';

// Create Axios instance
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000', // no duplicate /api
});

// Attach token interceptor
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named API functions
export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/users/register', userData);
  return response.data;
};

export const loginUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/users/login', userData);
  return response.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get('/api/projects');
  return response.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/projects', projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export axios instance for flexibility
export default api;
```

```javascript
  return config;

  return data;

  const { data } = await api.get("/api/projects");
  return data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const { data } = await api.post("/api/projects", projectData);
  return data;
};
```

```jsx
// src/pages/Login.jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom';
import { loginUser } from '../utils/api';
import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext';

const Login = () => {
  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ email: '', password: '' });
  const { setAuthData } = useAuth();
  const navigate = useNavigate();

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    setFormData({ ...formData, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await loginUser(formData);
      localStorage.setItem('token', data.token);
      setAuthData(data);
      navigate('/dashboard');
    } catch (err) {
      alert('Login failed');
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="login-container">
      <h2>Login</h2>
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <input type="email" name="email" placeholder="Email" onChange={handleChange} required />
        <input type="password" name="password" placeholder="Password" onChange={handleChange} required />
        <button type="submit">Login</button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};
```

```jsx
  useEffect(() => {
    const fetchProjects = async () => {
      try {
        const data = await getProjects();
        setProjects(data);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error('Failed to fetch projects:', err);
      }
    };

    fetchProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="dashboard">
      <h1>Dashboard</h1>
      <AddProjectForm onProjectCreated={setProjects} />
      <ul>
        {projects.map((project) => (
```

```jsx
// src/pages/AddProjectForm.jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { createProject } from '../utils/api';

const AddProjectForm = ({ onProjectCreated }) => {
  const [projectName, setProjectName] = useState('');

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const newProject = await createProject({ name: projectName });
      setProjectName('');
      onProjectCreated(prev => [...prev, newProject]);
    } catch (err) {
      console.error('Error creating project:', err);
    }
  };

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
      <input
        type="text"
        value={projectName}
        onChange={(e) => setProjectName(e.target.value)}
        placeholder="Project Name"
        required
      />
      <button type="submit">Add Project</button>
    </form>
  );
};

export default AddProjectForm;
```

```js
import axios from 'axios';

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000', // Adjust as needed for deployment
});

// Attach token automatically to all requests
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const userData = localStorage.getItem('user');
  const token = userData ? JSON.parse(userData).token : null;

  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }

  return config;
});

// Named API function exports
export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post('/api/users/login', credentials);
  return res.data;
};

export const registerUser = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post('/api/users/register', data);
  return res.data;
};

  const res = await api.get('/api/projects');
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const res = await api.post('/api/projects', projectData);
  return res.data;
};

// Export configured axios instance if needed elsewhere
export { api };
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
Here is the updated **`AuthContext.jsx`** implementation with safe JSON parsing from `localStorage` and appropriate fallback defaults:

---

### ✅ `src/context/AuthContext.jsx`

```jsx
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from 'react';

const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const getInitialUser = () => {
    try {
      const storedUser = localStorage.getItem('user');
      return storedUser ? JSON.parse(storedUser) : null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  };

  const getInitialToken = () => {
    try {
      return localStorage.getItem('token') || '';
    } catch {
      return '';
    }
  };

  const [user, setUser] = useState(getInitialUser);
  const [token, setToken] = useState(getInitialToken);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem('user');
    }

    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem('token', token);
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem('token');
    }
  }, [user, token]);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken('');
    localStorage.removeItem('user');
    localStorage.removeItem('token');
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => useContext(AuthContext);
```

---

**Changes implemented:**
- Wrapped `JSON.parse()` and `localStorage.getItem()` calls in `try/catch` for safety.
- Fallback to `null` for user and `''` for token if parsing fails or data is missing.
- `useEffect` keeps `localStorage` in sync with context state.
- Consistent state cleanup on logout.

This ensures robust handling of corrupted or missing `localStorage` data and avoids application crashes from invalid JSON.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
Here's the updated `AuthContext.jsx` with **safe localStorage parsing**, **fallback defaults**, and adjusted `useAuth` hook:

---

### ✅ `src/context/AuthContext.jsx`

```jsx
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from 'react';

// Utility to safely parse JSON from localStorage
const safeParse = (key, fallback) => {
  try {
    const value = localStorage.getItem(key);
    return value ? JSON.parse(value) : fallback;
  } catch {
    return fallback;
  }
};

const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse('user', null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => localStorage.getItem('token') || null);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(user));
    if (token) localStorage.setItem('token', token);
  }, [user, token]);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
    localStorage.removeItem('user');
    localStorage.removeItem('token');
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => useContext(AuthContext);
```

---

### ✅ Effects of this Update:
- Prevents frontend crash from corrupted JSON in `localStorage`.
- Fallbacks ensure empty/null state does not break rendering.
- `useAuth()` works exactly the same, now with safer defaults.

Let me know if you need the full updated context folder zipped or want to proceed with Dashboard/logic tests.

ASSISTANT:
Here's the refactored `utils/api.js` module with **named exports** for `loginUser`, `registerUser`, `getProjects`, and `createProject`, plus a default configured `axios` instance with baseURL and token interceptor:

---

### ✅ `frontend/src/utils/api.js`
```javascript
import axios from 'axios';

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000',
});

// Attach token to all requests
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const user = localStorage.getItem('user');
  if (user) {
    const { token } = JSON.parse(user);
    if (token) {
      config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
    }
  }
  return config;
});

// Auth endpoints
export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post('/api/auth/login', credentials);
  return res.data;
};

export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const res = await api.post('/api/auth/register', userData);
  return res.data;
};

// Project endpoints
export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get('/api/projects');
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const res = await api.post('/api/projects', projectData);
  return res.data;
};

export default api;
```

```js
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000',
});

// Attach token to every request
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const user = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('user')) || {};
  if (user.token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${user.token}`;
  }
```

```jsx
// src/pages/Login.jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom';
import { loginUser } from '../utils/api';

const Login = () => {
  const [email, setEmail] = useState('');
  const [password, setPassword] = useState('');
  const navigate = useNavigate();
  const [error, setError] = useState('');

  const handleLogin = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await loginUser({ email, password });
      localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(data));
      navigate('/dashboard');
    } catch (err) {
      setError('Login failed. Please check your credentials.');
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="login-container">
      <h2>Login</h2>
      <form onSubmit={handleLogin}>
        <input
          type="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          value={email}
          onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
          required
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          value={password}
          onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
          required
        />
        <button type="submit">Login</button>
        {error && <p className="error">{error}</p>}
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

```jsx
// src/pages/AddProjectForm.jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { createProject, getProjects } from '../utils/api';

const AddProjectForm = ({ onProjectAdded }) => {
  const [title, setTitle] = useState('');

  const handleAddProject = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    if (!title) return;
    try {
      await createProject({ title });
      const updated = await getProjects();
      onProjectAdded(updated);
      setTitle('');
    } catch (err) {
      console.error('Project creation failed:', err);
    }
  };

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleAddProject}>
      <input
        type="text"
        placeholder="New project title"
        value={title}
        onChange={(e) => setTitle(e.target.value)}
        required
      />
      <button type="submit">Add Project</button>
    </form>
  );
};

export default AddProjectForm;
```

```js
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from 'axios';

// Create axios instance with baseURL and token interceptor
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000',
});

// Attach token to every request if available
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named function to register a user
export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/users/register', userData);
  return response.data;
};

// Named function to log in a user
export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/users/login', credentials);
  return response.data;
};

// Named function to get projects
export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get('/api/projects');
  return response.data;
};

// Named function to create a new project
export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/projects', projectData);
  return response.data;
};
```

#### 1. **`utils/api.js` – Base URL and Token Handling**

```js
// utils/api.js
import axios from 'axios';

// Axios instance with baseURL and token interceptor
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000', // No /api suffix
});

// Add token to Authorization header if available
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const storedUser = localStorage.getItem('user');
  const token = storedUser ? JSON.parse(storedUser).token : null;
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named exports for API calls
export const registerUser = (userData) => api.post('/api/users/register', userData);
export const loginUser = (userData) => api.post('/api/users/login', userData);
export const getProjects = () => api.get('/api/projects');
export const createProject = (projectData) => api.post('/api/projects', projectData);

export default api;
```

---

#### 2. **`pages/Login.jsx` – Use `loginUser` properly**

```jsx
// pages/Login.jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom';
import { loginUser } from '../utils/api';
import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext';

const Login = () => {
  const { login } = useAuth();
  const navigate = useNavigate();
  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ email: '', password: '' });
  const [error, setError] = useState('');

  const handleChange = (e) =>
    setFormData({ ...formData, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const { data } = await loginUser(formData);
      login(data.token, data.user);
      navigate('/dashboard');
    } catch (err) {
      setError(err.response?.data?.message || 'Login failed');
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="container login-form">
      <h2>Login</h2>
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <input type="email" name="email" placeholder="Email" value={formData.email} onChange={handleChange} required />
        <input type="password" name="password" placeholder="Password" value={formData.password} onChange={handleChange} required />
        <button type="submit">Login</button>
      </form>
      {error && <p className="error">{error}</p>}
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

---

#### 3. **`pages/Dashboard.jsx` – Fetch projects after login**

```jsx
// pages/Dashboard.jsx
import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext';
import { getProjects } from '../utils/api';
import AddProjectForm from '../components/AddProjectForm';
import TaskList from '../components/TaskList';

const Dashboard = () => {
  const { user } = useAuth();
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);
  const [error, setError] = useState('');

  useEffect(() => {
    const fetchProjects = async () => {
      try {
        const { data } = await getProjects();
        setProjects(data);
      } catch (err) {
        setError('Failed to fetch projects');
      }
    };
    fetchProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="dashboard">
      <h2>Welcome, {user?.name || 'User'}</h2>
      <AddProjectForm onProjectCreated={setProjects} />
      {error && <p className="error">{error}</p>}
      <TaskList projects={projects} />
    </div>
  );
};

export default Dashboard;
```

```jsx
    try {
      await createProject({ title });

        required

};
```

ASSISTANT:
✅ **AuthContext Updated:** `AuthContext.js` and `useAuth` hook have been refactored to safely parse values from `localStorage` using fallback defaults.

---

### ✅ Updated `src/context/AuthContext.jsx`

```jsx
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from 'react';

const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [auth, setAuth] = useState({
    token: null,
    user: null,
  });

  useEffect(() => {
    const storedAuth = localStorage.getItem('auth');
    if (storedAuth) {
      try {
        const parsedAuth = JSON.parse(storedAuth);
        setAuth({
          token: parsedAuth.token || null,
          user: parsedAuth.user || null,
        });
      } catch (e) {
        setAuth({ token: null, user: null });
      }
    }
  }, []);

  const login = (userData) => {
    localStorage.setItem('auth', JSON.stringify(userData));
    setAuth(userData);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    localStorage.removeItem('auth');
    setAuth({ token: null, user: null });
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ auth, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => {
  return useContext(AuthContext);
};
```

---

**✅ Improvements Applied:**

- `try/catch` block ensures invalid JSON won’t crash the app.
- Defaults `token: null`, `user: null` when no data or invalid JSON.
- `useAuth` remains a clean consumer interface.

🔁 Now ready for consistent usage across `Login.jsx`, `Dashboard.jsx`, and all protected routes.

### ✅ Refactored `utils/api.js`

```js
// utils/api.js
import axios from 'axios';

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000',
});

// Attach token to every request if it exists
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const authData = localStorage.getItem('auth');
  if (authData) {
    const parsed = JSON.parse(authData);
    if (parsed?.token) {
      config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${parsed.token}`;
    }
  }
  return config;
});

// Named functions
export const login = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/auth/login', credentials);
  return response.data;
};

export const registerUser = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/auth/register', credentials);
  return response.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get('/api/projects');
  return response.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/projects', projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export the configured axios instance
export default api;
```

---

### ✅ `frontend/src/utils/api.js`
```js
import axios from 'axios';

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:5000',
});

// Attach token interceptor
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const userData = localStorage.getItem('user');
  if (userData) {
    const { token } = JSON.parse(userData);
    if (token) {
      config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
    }
  }
  return config;
});

// === NAMED EXPORTS ===
export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/users/register', userData);
  return response.data;
};

export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/users/login', credentials);
  return response.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get('/api/projects');
  return response.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post('/api/projects', projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export the configured axios instance if needed
export { api };
```

```javascript
// frontend/src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000", // no extra /api prefix
```

### `frontend/src/pages/Login.jsx`
```javascript
// frontend/src/pages/Login.jsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { useNavigate } from "react-router-dom";
import { loginUser } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Login = () => {
  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ email: "", password: "" });
  const [error, setError] = useState("");
  const navigate = useNavigate();
  const { login } = useAuth();

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    setFormData((prev) => ({ ...prev, [e.target.name]: e.target.value }));
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await loginUser(formData);
      login(data.token, data.user);
      navigate("/dashboard");
    } catch (err) {
      setError("Invalid credentials, please try again.");
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center bg-gray-900">
      <div className="bg-gray-800 p-8 rounded-lg shadow-lg w-full max-w-sm">
        <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-center text-white mb-6">Login</h2>
        {error && <p className="text-red-500 text-center mb-4">{error}</p>}
        <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="space-y-4">
          <input
            type="email"
            name="email"
            placeholder="Email"
            onChange={handleChange}
            className="w-full p-2 rounded bg-gray-700 text-white focus:outline-none"
          />
          <input
            type="password"
            name="password"
            placeholder="Password"
            onChange={handleChange}
            className="w-full p-2 rounded bg-gray-700 text-white focus:outline-none"
          />
          <button
            type="submit"
            className="w-full py-2 px-4 bg-blue-500 hover:bg-blue-600 rounded text-white"
          >
            Login
          </button>
        </form>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

### `frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx`
```javascript
// frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx
import { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";
import { getProjects } from "../utils/api";
import AddProjectForm from "../components/AddProjectForm";

const Dashboard = () => {
  const { user } = useAuth();
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const fetchProjects = async () => {
      try {
        const data = await getProjects();
        setProjects(data);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error("Error fetching projects:", err);
      }
    };
    fetchProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-gray-900 text-white p-6">
      <h1 className="text-3xl font-bold mb-6">Welcome, {user?.name}</h1>

      <AddProjectForm onProjectCreated={(newProject) => setProjects([...projects, newProject])} />

      <h2 className="text-2xl font-semibold mt-8 mb-4">Your Projects</h2>
      <ul className="space-y-2">
        {projects.map((project) => (
          <li key={project._id} className="bg-gray-800 p-3 rounded">
            {project.name}
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Dashboard;
```

### `frontend/src/components/AddProjectForm.jsx`
```javascript
// frontend/src/components/AddProjectForm.jsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { createProject } from "../utils/api";

const AddProjectForm = ({ onProjectCreated }) => {
  const [projectName, setProjectName] = useState("");

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    if (!projectName.trim()) return;

    try {
      const newProject = await createProject({ name: projectName });
      onProjectCreated(newProject);
      setProjectName("");
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("Error creating project:", err);
    }
  };

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="flex space-x-2 mb-6">
      <input
        type="text"
        value={projectName}
        onChange={(e) => setProjectName(e.target.value)}
        placeholder="New project name"
        className="flex-1 p-2 rounded bg-gray-700 text-white focus:outline-none"
      />
      <button
        type="submit"
        className="px-4 py-2 bg-green-500 hover:bg-green-600 rounded text-white"
      >
        Add
      </button>
    </form>
  );
};

export default AddProjectForm;
```
