```javascript
// frontend/src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000/api",
});

// Attach Authorization token automatically if available
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Register new user
export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/auth/register", userData);
  return response.data;
};

// Login user
export const loginUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/auth/login", userData);
  return response.data;
};

// Get all projects (requires token)
export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get("/projects");
  return response.data;
};

// Create a new project (requires token)
export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export the axios instance in case we need custom requests
export default api;
```

This setup guarantees:  
- **Named exports** for all API calls.  
- **Automatic token injection** into `Authorization` header.  
- **Consistent baseURL** (`http://localhost:5000/api`) without duplicate prefixes.

ASSISTANT:

```javascript
// utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with baseURL
const axiosInstance = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000", // Adjust if deploying
});

// Attach Authorization header if token exists
axiosInstance.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
  if (token) {
    config.headers["Authorization"] = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named API functions
export const loginUser = async (email, password) => {
  const response = await axiosInstance.post("/api/users/login", { email, password });
  return response.data;
};

export const registerUser = async (name, email, password) => {
  const response = await axiosInstance.post("/api/users/register", { name, email, password });
  return response.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await axiosInstance.get("/api/projects");
  return response.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await axiosInstance.post("/api/projects", projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export axiosInstance for advanced use
export { axiosInstance };
```

This structure ensures:  
- Consistent **baseURL** (`http://localhost:5000`).  
- No duplicate `/api` prefixes in components.  
- Automatic **Authorization header** if token is stored.  
- Named exports that match component imports (`loginUser`, `registerUser`, `getProjects`, `createProject`).

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the updated **`AuthContext.js`** with safe JSON parsing for values from `localStorage`, fallback defaults, and a clean `useAuth` hook:  

```jsx
// src/context/AuthContext.js
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext(null);

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  // Safe parse utility
  const safeParse = (key, fallback) => {
    try {
      const stored = localStorage.getItem(key);
      return stored ? JSON.parse(stored) : fallback;
    } catch {
      return fallback;
    }
  };

  // Initialize state with safe parsing
  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse("user", null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => {
    try {
      return localStorage.getItem("token") || null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  });

  // Persist changes to localStorage
  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("user");
    }
  }, [user]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem("token", token);
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("token");
    }
  }, [token]);

  const login = (userData, tokenValue) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(tokenValue);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
    localStorage.removeItem("user");
    localStorage.removeItem("token");
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

// Custom hook to consume AuthContext
export const useAuth = () => {
  const context = useContext(AuthContext);
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useAuth must be used within an AuthProvider");
  }
  return context;
};
```

This ensures:  
- Values from `localStorage` are parsed safely.  
- If parsing fails (corrupted JSON), fallback values are used.  
- `useAuth` hook remains clean and reliable.  

Do you want me to also **update imports across `Login.jsx`, `Dashboard.jsx`, and `AddProjectForm.jsx`** to align with this new safe AuthContext?

USER:
Refactor the API utility module to use named exports for login, registerUser, getProjects, createProject, attach auth token via interceptor, and remove default export.

ASSISTANT:
Here is the refactored `utils/api.js` with **named exports only**, proper **baseURL**, and an **axios interceptor** for attaching the `Authorization` header:  

```javascript
// utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000/api",
});

// Attach auth token to every request if available
api.interceptors.request.use(
  (config) => {
    const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
    if (token) {
      config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
    }
    return config;
  },
  (error) => Promise.reject(error)
);

// Named exports for API actions
export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/users/register", userData);
  return response.data;
};

export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post("/users/login", credentials);
  return response.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get("/projects");
  return response.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export axios instance for optional direct use
export { api };
```

### Key Changes
- Removed **default export** (everything is now named).
- Added **axios interceptor** to include `Authorization: Bearer <token>` automatically.
- Clean `baseURL` → `http://localhost:5000/api`.
- Removed duplicate `/api` prefixes from function calls.

#### 1. **AuthContext (frontend/context/AuthContext.jsx)**
- Safely parse values from `localStorage`.
- Provide fallback defaults for missing/invalid tokens.
- Ensure `useAuth` hook is correctly exported/imported.

```jsx
import { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext(null);

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => {
    try {
      return localStorage.getItem("token") || null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  });

  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => {
    try {
      const storedUser = localStorage.getItem("user");
      return storedUser ? JSON.parse(storedUser) : null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  });

  useEffect(() => {
    if (token) localStorage.setItem("token", token);
    else localStorage.removeItem("token");
  }, [token]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user));
    else localStorage.removeItem("user");
  }, [user]);

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ token, setToken, user, setUser }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => useContext(AuthContext);
```

```jsx
  const [error, setError] = useState("");

  const navigate = useNavigate();

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    setFormData({ ...formData, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });
  };

      setUser(data.user);

      setError("Invalid credentials. Try again.");

      <form
        onSubmit={handleSubmit}

          className="w-full p-3 border rounded mb-4"

          type="password"
          name="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          onChange={handleChange}
          className="w-full p-3 border rounded mb-4"

          type="submit"
          className="w-full bg-blue-600 text-white p-3 rounded hover:bg-blue-700"
```

```javascript
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { createProject } from "../utils/api";

const AddProjectForm = ({ onProjectAdded }) => {
  const [name, setName] = useState("");

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const newProject = await createProject({ name });
      onProjectAdded(newProject);
      setName("");
    } catch (error) {
      console.error("Error creating project:", error);
    }
  };

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="flex gap-2">
      <input
        type="text"
        placeholder="New Project"
        value={name}
        onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)}
        className="border p-2 rounded flex-grow"
        required
      />
      <button
        type="submit"
        className="bg-green-600 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded hover:bg-green-700"
      >
        Add
      </button>
    </form>
  );
};

export default AddProjectForm;
```

Here’s the refactored **`utils/api.js`** module with named exports for `loginUser`, `getProjects`, `createProject`, plus `registerUser`, and a default `axios` instance that automatically attaches the token from `localStorage`:  

```javascript
// utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with base URL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000", // Backend server URL
});

// Interceptor to attach token if available
api.interceptors.request.use(
  (config) => {
    const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
    if (token) {
      config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
    }
    return config;
  },
  (error) => Promise.reject(error)
);

// ===== Named API functions =====
export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/api/auth/register", userData);
  return response.data;
};

export const loginUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/api/auth/login", userData);
  return response.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get("/api/projects");
  return response.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/api/projects", projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Default export for flexibility (axios instance with interceptor)
export default api;
```

- **Named exports only** for `registerUser`, `loginUser`, `getProjects`, `createProject`.  
- **Interceptor** ensures the `Authorization` header is attached for every request automatically.  
- **Default export** kept for axios instance in case components need direct usage.

### 1. **AuthContext Fixes**
- Ensure consistent named export/import of `AuthContext` and `useAuth`.  
- Safely parse `localStorage` with fallback defaults.

**`context/AuthContext.jsx`**
```jsx
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext(null);

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => {
    try {
      return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("user")) || null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  });
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => localStorage.getItem("token") || "");

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user));
    else localStorage.removeItem("user");
  }, [user]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (token) localStorage.setItem("token", token);
    else localStorage.removeItem("token");
  }, [token]);

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, setUser, token, setToken }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => useContext(AuthContext);
```

```javascript
  return response.data;
};
```

#### `Login.jsx`
```javascript
// Login.jsx
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";
import { loginUser } from "../utils/api"; // ✅ Named import

const Login = () => {
  const { login } = useAuth();
  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ email: "", password: "" });
  const [error, setError] = useState("");

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    setFormData({ ...formData, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await loginUser(formData);
      login(data.token, data.user);
    } catch {
      setError("Invalid credentials");
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="login-container">
      <h2>Login</h2>
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        {error && <p className="error">{error}</p>}
        <input
          type="email"
          name="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          value={formData.email}
          onChange={handleChange}
          required
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          name="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          value={formData.password}
          onChange={handleChange}
          required
        />
        <button type="submit">Login</button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

---

#### `Dashboard.jsx`
```javascript
// Dashboard.jsx
import React, { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { getProjects } from "../utils/api"; // ✅ Named import
import AddProjectForm from "./AddProjectForm";

const Dashboard = () => {
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

  const fetchProjects = async () => {
    try {
      const data = await getProjects();
      setProjects(data);
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("Error fetching projects:", err);
    }
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="dashboard-container">
      <h2>Dashboard</h2>
      <AddProjectForm onProjectAdded={fetchProjects} />
      <ul>
        {projects.map((project) => (
          <li key={project._id}>{project.name}</li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Dashboard;
```

```javascript
// AddProjectForm.jsx
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { createProject } from "../utils/api"; // ✅ Named import

const AddProjectForm = ({ onProjectAdded }) => {
  const [projectName, setProjectName] = useState("");

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    if (!projectName.trim()) return;

    try {
      await createProject({ name: projectName });
      setProjectName("");
      onProjectAdded();
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("Error creating project:", err);
    }
  };

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="add-project-form">
      <input
        type="text"
        placeholder="Project name"
        value={projectName}
        onChange={(e) => setProjectName(e.target.value)}
        required
      />
      <button type="submit">Add Project</button>
    </form>
  );
};

export default AddProjectForm;
```

Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the refactored **AuthContext.js** with safe parsing of `localStorage` values and updated `useAuth` hook:  

```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.js
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  // Safe localStorage parsing helper
  const safeParse = (key, defaultValue) => {
    try {
      const storedValue = localStorage.getItem(key);
      return storedValue ? JSON.parse(storedValue) : defaultValue;
    } catch {
      return defaultValue;
    }
  };

  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse("user", null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => localStorage.getItem("token") || null);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("user");
    }
  }, [user]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem("token", token);
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("token");
    }
  }, [token]);

  const login = (userData, userToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(userToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
    localStorage.removeItem("user");
    localStorage.removeItem("token");
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

// Updated hook
export const useAuth = () => {
  const context = useContext(AuthContext);
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useAuth must be used within an AuthProvider");
  }
  return context;
};
```

This ensures:  
- `localStorage` values are safely parsed with try/catch.  
- `user` defaults to `null` if parsing fails.  
- `token` defaults to `null` if not found.  
- `useAuth` throws an error if used outside `AuthProvider`.  

Would you like me to also refactor `Login.jsx` and `Dashboard.jsx` now to ensure they consume this updated context consistently?

```javascript
  return response.data;
```

### `Login.jsx`
```javascript
// src/components/Login.jsx
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";
import { loginUser } from "../utils/api";

const Login = () => {
  const { login } = useAuth();
  const [form, setForm] = useState({ email: "", password: "" });
  const [error, setError] = useState("");

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    setForm({ ...form, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await loginUser(form);
      login(data.token, data.user);
    } catch {
      setError("Invalid credentials, please try again.");
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="flex items-center justify-center min-h-screen bg-gray-900 text-white">
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="bg-gray-800 p-8 rounded shadow-md w-96">
        <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold mb-6 text-center">Login</h2>
        {error && <p className="text-red-500 mb-4">{error}</p>}
        <input
          type="email"
          name="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          value={form.email}
          onChange={handleChange}
          className="w-full p-2 mb-4 rounded bg-gray-700 border border-gray-600"
          required
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          name="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          value={form.password}
          onChange={handleChange}
          className="w-full p-2 mb-6 rounded bg-gray-700 border border-gray-600"
          required
        />
        <button
          type="submit"
          className="w-full bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-700 p-2 rounded font-semibold"
        >
          Login
        </button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

### `Dashboard.jsx`
```javascript
// src/components/Dashboard.jsx
import React, { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";
import { getProjects } from "../utils/api";
import AddProjectForm from "./AddProjectForm";

const Dashboard = () => {
  const { user } = useAuth();
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const fetchProjects = async () => {
      try {
        const data = await getProjects();
        setProjects(data);
      } catch (error) {
        console.error("Error fetching projects:", error);
      }
    };
    fetchProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="p-8 bg-gray-900 min-h-screen text-white">
      <h1 className="text-3xl font-bold mb-6">Welcome, {user?.email}</h1>
      <AddProjectForm onProjectCreated={(newProj) => setProjects([...projects, newProj])} />
      <div className="mt-8">
        <h2 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-4">Projects</h2>
        <ul className="space-y-2">
          {projects.map((proj) => (
            <li key={proj._id} className="bg-gray-800 p-4 rounded">
              {proj.name}
            </li>
          ))}
        </ul>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Dashboard;
```

```javascript
const AddProjectForm = ({ onProjectCreated }) => {

      setName("");

      console.error("Error creating project:", error);
    }

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="flex gap-2 mb-6">
```

```javascript
// frontend/src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000", // backend base URL
});

// Attach Authorization header if token exists
api.interceptors.request.use(
  (config) => {
    const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
    if (token) {
      config.headers["Authorization"] = `Bearer ${token}`;
    }
    return config;
  },
  (error) => Promise.reject(error)
);

// --- API Functions ---

export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/api/auth/register", userData);
  return response.data;
};

export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post("/api/auth/login", credentials);
  return response.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get("/api/projects");
  return response.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/api/projects", projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export axios instance (optional, for custom calls)
export default api;
```

This ensures:
- No duplicate `/api` prefixes in components.  
- Each function matches the imports you’re using (`registerUser`, `loginUser`, `getProjects`, `createProject`).

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the refactored `AuthContext.js` with safe parsing from `localStorage`, fallback defaults, and a consistent `useAuth` hook:  

```jsx
// src/context/AuthContext.js
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext(null);

// Helper to safely parse JSON
const safeJSONParse = (key, fallback) => {
  try {
    const item = localStorage.getItem(key);
    return item ? JSON.parse(item) : fallback;
  } catch {
    return fallback;
  }
};

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeJSONParse("user", null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => localStorage.getItem("token") || "");

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("user");
    }

    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem("token", token);
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("token");
    }
  }, [user, token]);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken("");
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

// Hook for easy usage
export const useAuth = () => {
  const context = useContext(AuthContext);
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useAuth must be used within an AuthProvider");
  }
  return context;
};
```

### Key changes:
- Added `safeJSONParse` to handle malformed or missing JSON values in `localStorage`.
- Provided fallback defaults (`null` for `user`, `""` for `token`).
- Kept `useAuth` strict, ensuring it only runs inside an `AuthProvider`.

Would you like me to also **update the API utility** to automatically pull the `token` from this context instead of `localStorage`, so all API calls stay consistent?

```javascript
// utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000", // Backend base URL
});

// Attach Authorization header via interceptor
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
  if (token) {
    config.headers["Authorization"] = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named API helper functions
export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post("/login", credentials);
  return response.data;
};

export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/register", userData);
  return response.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get("/projects");
  return response.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export default axios instance for flexibility
export default api;
```

- Removed duplicate `/api` prefixes.

### `utils/api.js`
```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000", // Correct base URL
});

// Attach token automatically
api.interceptors.request.use(
  (config) => {
    const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
    if (token) {
      config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
    }
    return config;
  },
  (error) => Promise.reject(error)
);

// Named exports
export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post("/login", credentials);
  return response.data;
};

export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/register", userData);
  return response.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get("/projects");
  return response.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return response.data;
```

### `Login.jsx`
```javascript
// src/components/Login.jsx
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { loginUser, registerUser } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Login = () => {
  const { login } = useAuth();
  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ email: "", password: "" });
  const [isRegister, setIsRegister] = useState(false);

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = isRegister
        ? await registerUser(formData)
        : await loginUser(formData);

      login(data.user, data.token);
    } catch (error) {
      console.error("Auth error:", error.response?.data || error.message);
    }
  };

  return (
    <div>
      <h2>{isRegister ? "Register" : "Login"}</h2>
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <input
          type="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          value={formData.email}
          onChange={(e) => setFormData({ ...formData, email: e.target.value })}
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          value={formData.password}
          onChange={(e) =>
            setFormData({ ...formData, password: e.target.value })
          }
        />
        <button type="submit">{isRegister ? "Register" : "Login"}</button>
      </form>
      <button onClick={() => setIsRegister(!isRegister)}>
        {isRegister ? "Already have an account? Login" : "No account? Register"}
      </button>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

### `Dashboard.jsx`

```javascript
// src/components/Dashboard.jsx
import React, { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { getProjects } from "../utils/api";

import AddProjectForm from "./AddProjectForm";

const Dashboard = () => {
  const { user } = useAuth();
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

  const loadProjects = async () => {
    try {
      const data = await getProjects();
      setProjects(data);
    } catch (error) {
      console.error("Failed to load projects:", error.response?.data || error.message);
    }
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    loadProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div>
      <h2>Welcome, {user?.email}</h2>
      <AddProjectForm onProjectCreated={loadProjects} />
      <ul>
        {projects.map((project) => (
          <li key={project._id}>{project.name}</li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Dashboard;
```

```javascript
      setName("");
```

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the corrected setup, covering **AuthContext exports/imports**, **API utility exports**, **Authorization headers**, and **consistent styles** for **Login** and **Dashboard**.  

---

### 1. AuthContext (`src/context/AuthContext.js`)
```javascript
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext(null);

// Safe parse helper
const safeParse = (key, fallback) => {
  try {
    const item = localStorage.getItem(key);
    return item ? JSON.parse(item) : fallback;
  } catch {
    return fallback;
  }
};

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse("user", null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => localStorage.getItem("token") || "");

  useEffect(() => {
    user
      ? localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user))
      : localStorage.removeItem("user");
    token
      ? localStorage.setItem("token", token)
      : localStorage.removeItem("token");
  }, [user, token]);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken("");
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

// Named export hook
export const useAuth = () => {
  const context = useContext(AuthContext);
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useAuth must be used within an AuthProvider");
  }
  return context;
};
```

---

### 2. API Utility (`src/utils/api.js`)
```javascript
import axios from "axios";

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

// Attach Authorization header
api.interceptors.request.use(
  (config) => {
    const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
    if (token) {
      config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
    }
    return config;
  },
  (error) => Promise.reject(error)
);

// Named API calls
export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post("/login", credentials);
  return res.data;
};

export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const res = await api.post("/register", userData);
  return res.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get("/projects");
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const res = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return res.data;
};

export { api };
```

---

### 3. Login Page (`src/pages/Login.jsx`)
```javascript
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { loginUser } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Login = () => {
  const { login } = useAuth();
  const [form, setForm] = useState({ email: "", password: "" });
  const [error, setError] = useState("");

  const handleChange = (e) =>
    setForm({ ...form, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await loginUser(form);
      login(data.user, data.token);
    } catch {
      setError("Invalid email or password");
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="flex items-center justify-center h-screen bg-gray-100">
      <form
        onSubmit={handleSubmit}
        className="bg-white p-6 rounded-2xl shadow-md w-96"
      >
        <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold mb-4 text-center">Login</h2>
        {error && (
          <div className="text-red-500 text-sm mb-3 text-center">{error}</div>
        )}
        <input
          type="email"
          name="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          value={form.email}
          onChange={handleChange}
          className="w-full border p-2 mb-3 rounded"
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          name="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          value={form.password}
          onChange={handleChange}
          className="w-full border p-2 mb-4 rounded"
        />
        <button
          type="submit"
          className="w-full bg-blue-600 text-white p-2 rounded hover:bg-blue-700 transition"
        >
          Login
        </button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

---

### 4. Dashboard Page (`src/pages/Dashboard.jsx`)
```javascript
import React, { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { getProjects } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Dashboard = () => {
  const { user, logout } = useAuth();
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const fetchProjects = async () => {
      try {
        const data = await getProjects();
        setProjects(data);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error("Failed to load projects", err);
      }
    };
    fetchProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-gray-100 p-6">
      <div className="flex justify-between items-center mb-6">
        <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold">Welcome, {user?.name}</h1>
        <button
          onClick={logout}
          className="bg-red-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded hover:bg-red-600 transition"
        >
          Logout
        </button>
      </div>
      <div className="bg-white p-6 rounded-2xl shadow-md">
        <h2 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-4">Your Projects</h2>
        {projects.length === 0 ? (
          <p className="text-gray-500">No projects found.</p>
        ) : (
          <ul className="space-y-2">
            {projects.map((p) => (
              <li
                key={p._id}
                className="p-3 bg-gray-50 border rounded-lg shadow-sm"
              >
                {p.name}
              </li>
            ))}
          </ul>
        )}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Dashboard;
```

```javascript
  (config) => {
    const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
    if (token) {

    return config;
  },
  (error) => Promise.reject(error)
);

  return res.data;
```

### `src/pages/Dashboard.jsx`

```javascript
import React, { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { getProjects } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";
import AddProjectForm from "../components/AddProjectForm";

const Dashboard = () => {
  const { user, logout } = useAuth();
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

  const loadProjects = async () => {
    try {
      const data = await getProjects();
      setProjects(data);
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("Failed to load projects", err);
    }
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    loadProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-gray-100 p-6">
      <div className="flex justify-between items-center mb-6">
        <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold">Welcome, {user?.email}</h1>
        <button
          onClick={logout}
          className="bg-red-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded hover:bg-red-600 transition"
        >
          Logout
        </button>
      </div>
      <AddProjectForm onProjectCreated={loadProjects} />
      <div className="bg-white p-6 rounded-2xl shadow-md mt-6">
        <h2 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-4">Your Projects</h2>
        {projects.length === 0 ? (
          <p className="text-gray-500">No projects found.</p>
        ) : (
          <ul className="space-y-2">
            {projects.map((p) => (
              <li
                key={p._id}
                className="p-3 bg-gray-50 border rounded-lg shadow-sm"
              >
                {p.name}
              </li>
            ))}
          </ul>
        )}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Dashboard;
```

### `src/components/AddProjectForm.jsx`
```javascript
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { createProject } from "../utils/api";

const AddProjectForm = ({ onProjectCreated }) => {
  const [name, setName] = useState("");

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      await createProject({ name });
      setName("");
      onProjectCreated();
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("Failed to create project", err);
    }
  };

  return (
    <form
      onSubmit={handleSubmit}
      className="flex items-center gap-2 bg-white p-4 rounded-2xl shadow-md"
    >
      <input
        type="text"
        placeholder="Project name"
        value={name}
        onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)}
        className="flex-1 border p-2 rounded"
      />
      <button
        type="submit"
        className="bg-green-600 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded hover:bg-green-700 transition"
      >
        Add
      </button>
    </form>
  );
};

export default AddProjectForm;
```

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.jsx
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(null);
  const [token, setToken] = useState(null);

  // Safely parse JSON from localStorage
  const safeParse = (key, fallback = null) => {
    try {
      const value = localStorage.getItem(key);
      return value ? JSON.parse(value) : fallback;
    } catch {
      return fallback;
    }
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    const storedUser = safeParse("user", null);
    const storedToken = localStorage.getItem("token") || null;
    setUser(storedUser);
    setToken(storedToken);
  }, []);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
    localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(userData));
    localStorage.setItem("token", authToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
    localStorage.removeItem("user");
    localStorage.removeItem("token");
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

// Custom hook for using auth context
export const useAuth = () => {
  return useContext(AuthContext);
};
```

Refactor the API utility module to use named exports for login, registerUser, getProjects, createProject, attach auth token via interceptor, and remove default export.

```javascript
// src/utils/api.js

  (config) => {
    const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
    if (token) {
      config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
    }
    return config;
  },
  (error) => Promise.reject(error)
);

// Named API functions
export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const res = await api.post("/register", userData);
  return res.data;
};

export const login = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post("/login", credentials);
  return res.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get("/projects");
  return res.data;
};

  const res = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return res.data;
};
```  

### `src/context/AuthContext.jsx`
```javascript
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(null);
  const [token, setToken] = useState(null);

  const safeParse = (key, fallback = null) => {
    try {
      const value = localStorage.getItem(key);
      return value ? JSON.parse(value) : fallback;
    } catch {
      return fallback;
    }
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    setUser(safeParse("user", null));
    setToken(localStorage.getItem("token") || null);
  }, []);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
    localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(userData));
    localStorage.setItem("token", authToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
    localStorage.removeItem("user");
    localStorage.removeItem("token");
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => useContext(AuthContext);
```

---

```javascript
import axios from "axios";

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

api.interceptors.request.use(
  (config) => {
    const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
    if (token) {
      config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
    }
    return config;
  },
  (error) => Promise.reject(error)
);

export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const res = await api.post("/register", userData);
  return res.data;
};

export const login = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post("/login", credentials);
  return res.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get("/projects");
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const res = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return res.data;
};
```

```javascript
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { login } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Login = () => {
  const { login: loginUser } = useAuth();
  const [form, setForm] = useState({ email: "", password: "" });
  const [error, setError] = useState("");

  const handleChange = (e) =>
    setForm({ ...form, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await login(form);
      loginUser(data.user, data.token);
    } catch {
      setError("Invalid email or password");
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="flex items-center justify-center h-screen bg-gray-100">
      <form
        onSubmit={handleSubmit}
        className="bg-white p-6 rounded-2xl shadow-md w-96"
      >
        <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold mb-4 text-center">Login</h2>
        {error && (
          <div className="text-red-500 text-sm mb-3 text-center">{error}</div>
        )}
        <input
          type="email"
          name="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          value={form.email}
          onChange={handleChange}
          className="w-full border p-2 mb-3 rounded"
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          name="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          value={form.password}
          onChange={handleChange}
          className="w-full border p-2 mb-4 rounded"
        />
        <button
          type="submit"
          className="w-full bg-blue-600 text-white p-2 rounded-2xl shadow hover:bg-blue-700 transition"
        >
          Login
        </button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000/api",
});

// Interceptor: attach Authorization header if token exists
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named exports for API functions
export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/auth/register", userData);
  return response.data;
};

export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return response.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get("/projects");
  return response.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export the axios instance too (for flexibility)
export { api };
```

- All functions (`registerUser`, `loginUser`, `getProjects`, `createProject`) are exported as **named functions**.  
- No default export is used.

```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with backend baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000", // backend root URL
});

// Interceptor to attach token automatically
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named API functions
export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/register", userData);
  return res.data;
};

export const loginUser = async (userData) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/login", userData);
  return res.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get("/projects");
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const res = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return res.data;
};

export default api;
```

### `src/pages/Login.jsx`
```javascript
// src/pages/Login.jsx
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { useNavigate } from "react-router-dom";
import { loginUser } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Login = () => {
  const [email, setEmail] = useState("");
  const [password, setPassword] = useState("");
  const { login } = useAuth();
  const navigate = useNavigate();

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await loginUser({ email, password });
      login(data.token, data.user); // Save token + user
      navigate("/dashboard");
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("Login failed:", err.response?.data || err.message);
      alert("Login failed. Please check your credentials.");
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center bg-gray-100">
      <form
        onSubmit={handleSubmit}
        className="bg-white p-6 rounded shadow-md w-96"
      >
        <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold mb-4">Login</h2>
        <input
          type="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          value={email}
          onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
          className="w-full p-2 mb-3 border rounded"
          required
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          value={password}
          onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
          className="w-full p-2 mb-3 border rounded"
          required
        />
        <button
          type="submit"
          className="w-full bg-blue-500 text-white p-2 rounded hover:bg-blue-600"
        >
          Login
        </button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

---

### `src/pages/Dashboard.jsx`
```javascript
// src/pages/Dashboard.jsx
import React, { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { getProjects } from "../utils/api";
import AddProjectForm from "./AddProjectForm";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Dashboard = () => {
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);
  const { user } = useAuth();

  useEffect(() => {
    const fetchProjects = async () => {
      try {
        const data = await getProjects();
        setProjects(data);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error("Failed to fetch projects:", err);
      }
    };
    fetchProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="p-6">
      <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold mb-4">
        Welcome, {user?.name || "User"}
      </h1>
      <AddProjectForm onProjectAdded={(p) => setProjects([...projects, p])} />
      <ul className="mt-4 space-y-2">
        {projects.map((project) => (
          <li
            key={project._id}
            className="p-3 border rounded shadow-sm bg-white"
          >
            {project.name}
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Dashboard;
```

```javascript
// src/pages/AddProjectForm.jsx
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { createProject } from "../utils/api";

const AddProjectForm = ({ onProjectAdded }) => {
  const [projectName, setProjectName] = useState("");

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const newProject = await createProject({ name: projectName });
      onProjectAdded(newProject);
      setProjectName("");
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("Failed to create project:", err.response?.data || err.message);
      alert("Error creating project.");
    }
  };

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="flex gap-2 mb-4">
      <input
        type="text"
        placeholder="New Project"
        value={projectName}
        onChange={(e) => setProjectName(e.target.value)}
        className="flex-grow p-2 border rounded"
        required
      />
      <button
        type="submit"
        className="bg-green-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded hover:bg-green-600"
      >
        Add
      </button>
    </form>
  );
};

export default AddProjectForm;
```

```javascript
// frontend/src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with base URL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000/api",
});

// Attach Authorization header automatically if token exists
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

  const response = await api.post("/users/register", userData);
  return response.data;
};

// Login user
export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post("/users/login", credentials);
  return response.data;
};

// Fetch all projects for authenticated user
export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get("/projects");
  return response.data;
};

// Create a new project
export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export axios instance in case it's needed
export { api };
```

This ensures:
- All functions are exported as **named exports** (no default export anymore).  
- The `/api` prefix is included once in `baseURL`.  
- Every request automatically attaches the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header if available.

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the refactored `AuthContext.js` with **safe JSON parsing**, fallback defaults, and an adjusted `useAuth` hook:  

```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.js
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext();

// Safe JSON parse helper
const safeParse = (key, fallback) => {
  try {
    const item = localStorage.getItem(key);
    return item ? JSON.parse(item) : fallback;
  } catch {
    return fallback;
  }
};

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse("user", null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => localStorage.getItem("token") || null);

  // Persist user and token in localStorage
  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("user");
    }

    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem("token", token);
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("token");
    }
  }, [user, token]);

  const login = (userData, tokenValue) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(tokenValue);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
    localStorage.removeItem("user");
    localStorage.removeItem("token");
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

// Hook for consuming AuthContext
export const useAuth = () => {
  const context = useContext(AuthContext);
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useAuth must be used within an AuthProvider");
  }
  return context;
};
```

### Key changes:
- Introduced `safeParse` helper to prevent JSON parse errors when `localStorage` contains invalid or empty values.  
- Added fallback defaults (`null`) when parsing fails.  
- Adjusted `useAuth` to ensure context safety.  

This ensures the app won’t crash due to malformed or missing localStorage data.  

Do you want me to also update the **Login.jsx** and **Dashboard.jsx** files to align with this new safe AuthContext structure?

Refactor the API helper module to export named functions for login, getProjects, createProject, and default axios instance with baseURL and token interceptor.

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the refactored `utils/api.js` implementing **named exports** for `loginUser`, `getProjects`, `createProject`, and keeping the **axios instance with baseURL + token interceptor**:  

```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with base URL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000/api", // Adjust if needed for deployment
});

// Attach token interceptor to include Authorization header automatically
api.interceptors.request.use(
  (config) => {
    const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
    if (token) {
      config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
    }
    return config;
  },
  (error) => Promise.reject(error)
);

// ========== AUTH FUNCTIONS ==========
export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return response.data;
};

// If you have registration enabled
export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/auth/register", userData);
  return response.data;
};

// ========== PROJECT FUNCTIONS ==========
export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get("/projects");
  return response.data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export axios instance for custom calls
export default api;
```

### Key Changes:
- All **API functions are named exports** (`loginUser`, `registerUser`, `getProjects`, `createProject`).
- A single **axios instance** (`api`) with `baseURL: http://localhost:5000/api`.
- **Interceptor** ensures the token from `localStorage` is always attached.
- Default export (`api`) is kept only for custom/manual calls if needed.

### **1. `src/context/AuthContext.jsx`**  
Safe parsing from `localStorage`, consistent exports, and `useAuth` hook.  

```jsx
// src/context/AuthContext.jsx
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext(null);

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [auth, setAuth] = useState(() => {
    try {
      const stored = localStorage.getItem("auth");
      return stored ? JSON.parse(stored) : { token: null, user: null };
    } catch {
      return { token: null, user: null };
    }
  });

  useEffect(() => {
    localStorage.setItem("auth", JSON.stringify(auth));
  }, [auth]);

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ auth, setAuth }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => useContext(AuthContext);
```

### **2. `src/utils/api.js`**  
Refactor with named exports, no duplicate `/api`, interceptor for token.  

```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000/api", // unified base URL
});

// Attach token automatically
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const auth = localStorage.getItem("auth");
  if (auth) {
    try {
      const { token } = JSON.parse(auth);
      if (token) config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
    } catch {
      /* ignore parse errors */
    }
  }
  return config;
});

// Named exports
export const registerUser = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post("/users/register", data);
  return res.data;
};

export const loginUser = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post("/users/login", data);
  return res.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get("/projects");
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post("/projects", data);
  return res.data;
};

export default api;
```

```jsx
// src/pages/Login.jsx
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { useNavigate } from "react-router-dom";
import { loginUser } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Login = () => {
  const navigate = useNavigate();
  const { setAuth } = useAuth();
  const [form, setForm] = useState({ email: "", password: "" });
  const [error, setError] = useState("");

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    setForm({ ...form, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await loginUser(form);
      setAuth({ token: data.token, user: data.user });
      navigate("/dashboard");
    } catch {
      setError("Invalid login credentials");
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="flex items-center justify-center min-h-screen bg-gray-100">
      <div className="w-full max-w-md p-6 bg-white rounded-lg shadow-md">
        <h2 className="mb-6 text-2xl font-bold text-center text-gray-800">
          Login
        </h2>
        {error && <p className="mb-4 text-red-500">{error}</p>}
        <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="space-y-4">
          <input
            type="email"
            name="email"
            placeholder="Email"
            value={form.email}
            onChange={handleChange}
            className="w-full px-4 py-2 border rounded-lg"
          />
          <input
            type="password"
            name="password"
            placeholder="Password"
            value={form.password}
            onChange={handleChange}
            className="w-full px-4 py-2 border rounded-lg"
          />
          <button
            type="submit"
            className="w-full py-2 text-white bg-blue-600 rounded-lg hover:bg-blue-700"
          >
            Login
          </button>
        </form>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

```javascript
  return response.data;
```

### `src/utils/api.js`
```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with base URL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

// Interceptor to attach token
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Auth
export const registerUser = (userData) => api.post("/api/auth/register", userData);
export const loginUser = (userData) => api.post("/api/auth/login", userData);

// Projects
export const getProjects = () => api.get("/api/projects");
export const createProject = (projectData) => api.post("/api/projects", projectData);
```

---

### `src/pages/Login.jsx`
```javascript
// src/pages/Login.jsx
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { useNavigate } from "react-router-dom";
import { loginUser } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Login = () => {
  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ email: "", password: "" });
  const [error, setError] = useState("");
  const navigate = useNavigate();
  const { login } = useAuth();

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    setFormData({ ...formData, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const res = await loginUser(formData);
      login(res.data.token, res.data.user);
      navigate("/dashboard");
    } catch (err) {
      setError("Invalid credentials. Try again.");
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="flex items-center justify-center min-h-screen bg-gray-100">
      <form
        onSubmit={handleSubmit}
        className="bg-white p-6 rounded-xl shadow-md w-96"
      >
        <h2 className="text-2xl font-semibold mb-4 text-center">Login</h2>
        {error && <p className="text-red-500 text-sm mb-2">{error}</p>}
        <input
          type="email"
          name="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          className="w-full mb-3 p-2 border rounded"
          onChange={handleChange}
          required
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          name="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          className="w-full mb-3 p-2 border rounded"
          onChange={handleChange}
          required
        />
        <button
          type="submit"
          className="w-full bg-blue-600 text-white py-2 rounded hover:bg-blue-700 transition"
        >
          Login
        </button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

---

### `src/pages/Dashboard.jsx`
```javascript
// src/pages/Dashboard.jsx
import React, { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { getProjects } from "../utils/api";
import AddProjectForm from "./AddProjectForm";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Dashboard = () => {
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);
  const { user } = useAuth();

  useEffect(() => {
    const fetchProjects = async () => {
      try {
        const res = await getProjects();
        setProjects(res.data);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error("Error fetching projects", err);
      }
    };
    fetchProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="p-6">
      <h1 className="text-xl font-bold mb-4">Welcome, {user?.name}</h1>
      <AddProjectForm onProjectAdded={setProjects} />
      <div className="mt-6">
        <h2 className="text-lg font-semibold mb-2">Your Projects</h2>
        <ul className="space-y-2">
          {projects.map((p) => (
            <li key={p._id} className="p-3 bg-gray-100 rounded shadow">
              {p.name}
            </li>
          ))}
        </ul>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Dashboard;
```

---

### `src/pages/AddProjectForm.jsx`
```javascript
// src/pages/AddProjectForm.jsx
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { createProject, getProjects } from "../utils/api";

const AddProjectForm = ({ onProjectAdded }) => {
  const [projectName, setProjectName] = useState("");

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    if (!projectName.trim()) return;

    try {
      await createProject({ name: projectName });
      const res = await getProjects(); // Refresh project list
      onProjectAdded(res.data);
      setProjectName("");
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("Error creating project", err);
    }
  };

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="flex space-x-2 mb-4">
      <input
        type="text"
        value={projectName}
        onChange={(e) => setProjectName(e.target.value)}
        placeholder="New project name"
        className="flex-1 p-2 border rounded"
      />
      <button
        type="submit"
        className="bg-green-600 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded hover:bg-green-700 transition"
      >
        Add
      </button>
    </form>
  );
};

export default AddProjectForm;
```

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the refactored `src/utils/api.js` with **named exports** for `loginUser`, `registerUser`, `getProjects`, `createProject`, plus a default `axios` instance configured with baseURL and token interceptor:

```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000/api", // Adjust base path for backend routes
});

// Attach token automatically from localStorage if available
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named function: register a new user
export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/auth/register", userData);
  return response.data;
};

// Named function: login user
export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return response.data;
};

// Named function: get all projects
export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get("/projects");
  return response.data;
};

// Named function: create a new project
export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export axios instance for advanced custom usage
export default api;
```

### Key Adjustments:
- `api` instance has `baseURL: "http://localhost:5000/api"`.
- Interceptor automatically injects `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.
- Removed duplicate `/api` prefixes from function calls.
- Exported named functions (`registerUser`, `loginUser`, `getProjects`, `createProject`) for use in components.
- Default export (`api`) is still available if needed for direct custom calls.

```javascript
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000/api", // Adjust if your backend prefix differs
});

// Attach token from localStorage automatically
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named export: register new user

  const response = await api.post("/auth/register", userData);
  return response.data;
};

// Named export: login user

  const response = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return response.data;
};

// Named export: fetch projects
export const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get("/projects");
  return response.data;
};

// Named export: create project
export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return response.data;
};
```

```javascript
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",

  return config;
```

```javascript
// src/components/Login.jsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { loginUser } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Login = () => {
  const { setUser, setToken } = useAuth();
  const [email, setEmail] = useState("");
  const [password, setPassword] = useState("");
  const [error, setError] = useState("");

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await loginUser({ email, password });
      setUser(data.user);
      setToken(data.token);
      localStorage.setItem("token", data.token);
    } catch {
      setError("Invalid credentials");
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="login-container">
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <h2>Login</h2>
        {error && <p className="error">{error}</p>}
        <input
          type="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          value={email}
          onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
          required
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          value={password}
          onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
          required
        />
        <button type="submit">Login</button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.js
import { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext(null);

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  // Safely parse token from localStorage
  const getStoredToken = () => {
    try {
      const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
      return token ? JSON.parse(token) : null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  };

  // Safely parse user from localStorage
  const getStoredUser = () => {
    try {
      const user = localStorage.getItem("user");
      return user ? JSON.parse(user) : null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  };

  const [token, setToken] = useState(getStoredToken);
  const [user, setUser] = useState(getStoredUser);

  // Keep localStorage in sync when token changes
  useEffect(() => {
    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(token));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("token");
    }
  }, [token]);

  // Keep localStorage in sync when user changes
  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("user");
    }
  }, [user]);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

// Hook to consume AuthContext safely
export const useAuth = () => {
  const context = useContext(AuthContext);
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useAuth must be used within an AuthProvider");
  }
  return context;
};
```  

This refactor ensures:  
- `localStorage` values are **safely parsed** with `try/catch`.  
- If corrupted, falls back to `null` defaults.  
- `useAuth` hook throws an error if used outside `AuthProvider`.  
- Both `user` and `token` sync cleanly with `localStorage`.

USER:
The user needs fixes to AuthContext exports and imports, API utility exports, include Authorization headers in API calls, and restore consistent styles for Login and Dashboard according to prior design.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.js
import { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext(null);

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const getStoredToken = () => {
    try {
      return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token")) || null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  };

  const getStoredUser = () => {
    try {
      return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("user")) || null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  };

  const [token, setToken] = useState(getStoredToken);
  const [user, setUser] = useState(getStoredUser);

  useEffect(() => {
    token
      ? localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(token))
      : localStorage.removeItem("token");
  }, [token]);

  useEffect(() => {
    user
      ? localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user))
      : localStorage.removeItem("user");
  }, [user]);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => {
  const context = useContext(AuthContext);
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useAuth must be used within an AuthProvider");
  }
  return context;
};
```

```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${JSON.parse(token)}`;
  }
  return config;
});

export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const { data } = await api.post("/api/auth/register", userData);
  return data;
};

export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const { data } = await api.post("/api/auth/login", credentials);
  return data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const { data } = await api.get("/api/projects");
  return data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const { data } = await api.post("/api/projects", projectData);
  return data;
};

export default api;
```

```javascript
// src/components/Login.jsx

import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Login = () => {
  const { login } = useAuth();
  const [email, setEmail] = useState("");
  const [password, setPassword] = useState("");
  const [error, setError] = useState("");

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {

      login(data.user, data.token);

  return (

            value={email}
            onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
            required
          />
          <input
            type="password"
            placeholder="Password"
            className="w-full p-3 rounded-xl bg-gray-700 text-white focus:outline-none"
            value={password}
            onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
            required
          />
          <button
            type="submit"
            className="w-full bg-blue-500 hover:bg-blue-600 text-white font-semibold py-3 rounded-xl"
          >
            Login
          </button>
        </form>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

```javascript
// src/components/Dashboard.jsx
import { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { getProjects } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Dashboard = () => {
  const { user } = useAuth();
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

  useEffect(() => {
    (async () => {
      try {
        const data = await getProjects();
        setProjects(data);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error("Failed to fetch projects", err);
      }
    })();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-gray-900 p-8 text-white">
      <div className="max-w-4xl mx-auto">
        <h2 className="text-3xl font-bold mb-4">Welcome, {user?.name}</h2>
        <div className="bg-gray-800 p-6 rounded-2xl shadow-lg">
          <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-4">Your Projects</h3>
          {projects.length > 0 ? (
            <ul className="space-y-2">
              {projects.map((project) => (
                <li
                  key={project._id}
                  className="p-3 bg-gray-700 rounded-xl shadow-sm"
                >
                  {project.title}
                </li>
              ))}
            </ul>
          ) : (
            <p className="text-gray-400">No projects yet.</p>
          )}
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};
```
