```javascript
// src/components/Login.jsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { loginUser } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Login = () => {
  const { login } = useAuth();
  const [email, setEmail] = useState("");
  const [password, setPassword] = useState("");
  const [error, setError] = useState("");

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await loginUser({ email, password });
      login(data.user, data.token);
    } catch {
      setError("Invalid credentials");
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="flex items-center justify-center min-h-screen bg-gray-900">
      <div className="bg-gray-800 p-8 rounded-2xl shadow-lg w-96">
        <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-center text-white mb-6">Login</h2>
        {error && <p className="text-red-400 text-sm mb-4">{error}</p>}
        <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="space-y-4">
          <input
            type="email"
            placeholder="Email"
            className="w-full p-3 rounded-xl bg-gray-700 text-white focus:outline-none"
            value={email}
            onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
            required
          />
          <input
            type="password"
            placeholder="Password"
            className="w-full p-3 rounded-xl bg-gray-700 text-white focus:outline-none"
            value={password}
            onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
            required
          />
          <button
            type="submit"
            className="w-full bg-blue-500 hover:bg-blue-600 text-white font-semibold py-3 rounded-xl"
          >
            Login
          </button>
        </form>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

```javascript
// src/components/Dashboard.jsx
import { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { getProjects } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Dashboard = () => {
  const { user } = useAuth();
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

  useEffect(() => {
    (async () => {
      try {
        const data = await getProjects();
        setProjects(data);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error("Failed to fetch projects", err);
      }
    })();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-gray-900 p-8 text-white">
      <div className="max-w-4xl mx-auto">
        <h2 className="text-3xl font-bold mb-4">Welcome, {user?.name}</h2>
        <div className="bg-gray-800 p-6 rounded-2xl shadow-lg">
          <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-4">Your Projects</h3>
          {projects.length > 0 ? (
            <ul className="space-y-2">
              {projects.map((project) => (
                <li
                  key={project._id}
                  className="p-3 bg-gray-700 rounded-xl shadow-sm"
                >
                  {project.title}
                </li>
              ))}
            </ul>
          ) : (
            <p className="text-gray-400">No projects yet.</p>
          )}
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Dashboard;
```

```javascript
// src/components/AddProjectForm.jsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { createProject } from "../utils/api";

const AddProjectForm = ({ onProjectAdded }) => {
  const [title, setTitle] = useState("");
  const [description, setDescription] = useState("");

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const project = await createProject({ title, description });
      onProjectAdded(project);
      setTitle("");
      setDescription("");
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("Failed to create project", err);
    }
  };

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="bg-gray-800 p-6 rounded-2xl shadow-lg mt-6">
      <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold text-white mb-4">Add New Project</h3>
      <input
        type="text"
        placeholder="Project Title"
        className="w-full p-3 rounded-xl bg-gray-700 text-white mb-3 focus:outline-none"
        value={title}
        onChange={(e) => setTitle(e.target.value)}
        required
      />
      <textarea
        placeholder="Project Description"
        className="w-full p-3 rounded-xl bg-gray-700 text-white mb-3 focus:outline-none"
        value={description}
        onChange={(e) => setDescription(e.target.value)}
        required
      />
      <button
        type="submit"
        className="w-full bg-green-500 hover:bg-green-600 text-white font-semibold py-3 rounded-xl"
      >
        Add Project
      </button>
    </form>
  );
};

export default AddProjectForm;
```

- Imports match correctly.  
- Styles are consistent (dark background, rounded cards, Tailwind).

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with correct baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

// Interceptor: automatically include token in headers
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${JSON.parse(token)}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named functions
export const login = async (credentials) => {
  const { data } = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const { data } = await api.get("/projects");
  return data;
};

export const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const { data } = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return data;
};

// Default export axios instance
export default api;
```

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.js
import { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext(null);

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(null);
  const [token, setToken] = useState(null);

  // Safely parse values from localStorage
  useEffect(() => {
    try {
      const storedUser = localStorage.getItem("user");
      const storedToken = localStorage.getItem("token");

      setUser(storedUser ? JSON.parse(storedUser) : null);
      setToken(storedToken ? JSON.parse(storedToken) : null);
    } catch (e) {
      setUser(null);
      setToken(null);
    }
  }, []);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
    localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(userData));
    localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(authToken));
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
    localStorage.removeItem("user");
    localStorage.removeItem("token");
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

// Custom hook for consuming context
export const useAuth = () => {
  return useContext(AuthContext);
};
```  

This ensures `AuthContext` reads from `localStorage` safely with JSON parsing and defaults. The `useAuth` hook now cleanly exposes `{ user, token, login, logout }`.

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.jsx
import { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

// Helper to safely parse JSON from localStorage
const safeParse = (key, fallback = null) => {
  try {
    const item = localStorage.getItem(key);
    return item ? JSON.parse(item) : fallback;
  } catch {
    return fallback;
  }
};

const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse("user", null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => safeParse("token", null));

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("user");
    }
  }, [user]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(token));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("token");
    }
  }, [token]);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

// Hook for consuming AuthContext
export const useAuth = () => {
  return useContext(AuthContext);
};
```

This update ensures:
- LocalStorage values are parsed safely.
- If JSON parsing fails, `null` (or a fallback) is returned instead of breaking the app.
- `useAuth` hook stays the same but now relies on the improved `AuthContext`.  

Would you like me to also update your `utils/api.js` so it uses this context’s `token` instead of fetching directly from `localStorage`?

```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

// Request interceptor to add Authorization header
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem("token")
    ? JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token"))
    : null;
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named API functions
const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return response.data;
};

const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get("/projects");
  return response.data;
};

const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export both instance and functions
export { api, loginUser, getProjects, createProject };
```  

This version:  
- Provides **named exports** only.  
- Uses `http://localhost:5000` as baseURL (no duplicate `/api`).  
- Attaches `Authorization: Bearer <token>` automatically through interceptor.

USER:

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

// Request interceptor to add Authorization header
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem("token")
    ? JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token"))
    : null;
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named API functions
const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/auth/register", userData);
  return response.data;
};

const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return response.data;
};

  const response = await api.get("/projects");
  return response.data;
};

  const response = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return response.data;
};
```  

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

// Request interceptor to add Authorization header if token exists
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem("token")
    ? JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token"))
    : null;
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named API functions
const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/auth/register", userData);
  return response.data;
};

const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const response = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return response.data;
};

const getProjects = async () => {
  const response = await api.get("/projects");
  return response.data;
};

const createProject = async (projectData) => {
  const response = await api.post("/projects", projectData);
  return response.data;
};

// Export only named exports (no default)
export { api, registerUser, loginUser, getProjects, createProject };
```  

This refactor ensures:
- **No default export**.  
- **Named exports only**: `registerUser`, `loginUser`, `getProjects`, `createProject`.  
- Token automatically attached to all requests via interceptor.  
- `api` instance remains available for additional custom calls.  

Next automation turn should update component imports (`Login.jsx`, `Dashboard.jsx`, `AddProjectForm.jsx`) to align with these named exports.

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.jsx
import { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  // Safe JSON parse with fallback
  const safeParse = (key, fallback = null) => {
    try {
      const item = localStorage.getItem(key);
      return item ? JSON.parse(item) : fallback;
    } catch {
      return fallback;
    }
  };

  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse("user", null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => safeParse("token", null));

  // Persist user and token to localStorage when they change
  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("user");
    }
  }, [user]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(token));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("token");
    }
  }, [token]);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
    localStorage.removeItem("user");
    localStorage.removeItem("token");
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => useContext(AuthContext);
```  

This update ensures:
- LocalStorage values are **safely parsed** with `try/catch`.
- Fallbacks prevent crashes on malformed JSON.
- `useAuth` hook behavior remains unchanged but more robust.  

Would you like me to also generate the **task update entry** in `chatGPT_Todo.txt` for this change in the next automation turn?

   ```js
   // src/context/AuthContext.jsx
   import { createContext, useState } from "react";

   const AuthContext = createContext();

   const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
     const [user, setUser] = useState(null);
     return (
       <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, setUser }}>
         {children}
       </AuthContext.Provider>
     );
   };

   export { AuthContext, AuthProvider };
   ```

2. **API Utility Exports**
   - Maintain only **named exports** for all API methods.
   - Keep `Authorization` header injection via axios interceptor.

   ```js
   // src/utils/api.js
   import axios from "axios";

   const api = axios.create({
     baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
   });

   api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
     const token = localStorage.getItem("token")
       ? JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token"))
       : null;
     if (token) {
       config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
     }
     return config;
   });

   const registerUser = async (userData) => {
     const res = await api.post("/auth/register", userData);
     return res.data;
   };

   const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
     const res = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
     return res.data;
   };

   const getProjects = async () => {
     const res = await api.get("/projects");
     return res.data;
   };

   const createProject = async (data) => {
     const res = await api.post("/projects", data);
     return res.data;
   };

   export { registerUser, loginUser, getProjects, createProject };
   ```

3. **Consistent Styles (Login & Dashboard)**
   - Apply consistent **card layout, centered form, Tailwind classes**.
   - Keep spacing, rounded corners, shadow, and blue accent theme.

   ```jsx
   // src/components/Login.jsx
   import { useState, useContext } from "react";
   import { loginUser } from "../utils/api";
   import { AuthContext } from "../context/AuthContext";

   const Login = () => {
     const { setUser } = useContext(AuthContext);
     const [email, setEmail] = useState("");
     const [password, setPassword] = useState("");

     const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
       e.preventDefault();
       const data = await loginUser({ email, password });
       localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(data.token));
       setUser(data.user);
     };

     return (
       <div className="flex h-screen items-center justify-center bg-gray-100">
         <div className="bg-white p-8 rounded-2xl shadow-lg w-96">
           <h2 className="text-xl font-bold mb-4 text-center">Login</h2>
           <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="space-y-4">
             <input
               type="email"
               value={email}
               onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
               placeholder="Email"
               className="w-full border px-3 py-2 rounded-md focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500"
             />
             <input
               type="password"
               value={password}
               onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
               placeholder="Password"
               className="w-full border px-3 py-2 rounded-md focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500"
             />
             <button
               type="submit"
               className="w-full bg-blue-600 text-white py-2 rounded-md hover:bg-blue-700 transition"
             >
               Login
             </button>
           </form>
         </div>
       </div>
     );
   };

   export default Login;
   ```

   ```jsx
   // src/components/Dashboard.jsx
   import { useEffect, useState, useContext } from "react";
   import { getProjects } from "../utils/api";
   import { AuthContext } from "../context/AuthContext";

   const Dashboard = () => {
     const { user } = useContext(AuthContext);
     const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

     useEffect(() => {
       const fetchData = async () => {
         const data = await getProjects();
         setProjects(data);
       };
       fetchData();
     }, []);

     return (
       <div className="min-h-screen bg-gray-100 p-8">
         <div className="max-w-4xl mx-auto bg-white rounded-2xl shadow-lg p-6">
           <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold mb-6">
             Welcome, {user?.email || "User"}
           </h2>
           <h3 className="text-xl mb-4">Projects</h3>
           <ul className="space-y-2">
             {projects.map((p) => (
               <li
                 key={p._id}
                 className="border px-4 py-2 rounded-md shadow-sm bg-gray-50"
               >
                 {p.name}
               </li>
             ))}
           </ul>
         </div>
       </div>
     );
   };

   export default Dashboard;
   ```

{"updates":[{"pattern":".*","multiple":false,"replacement":"// src/context/AuthContext.jsx\nimport { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from \"react\";\n\nconst AuthContext = createContext();\n\nconst AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {\n  const [user, setUser] = useState(null);\n  const [token, setToken] = useState(null);\n\n  // Load token and user safely from localStorage on mount\n  useEffect(() => {\n    try {\n      const storedToken = localStorage.getItem(\"token\")\n        ? JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(\"token\"))\n        : null;\n      const storedUser = localStorage.getItem(\"user\")\n        ? JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(\"user\"))\n        : null;\n\n      if (storedToken) setToken(storedToken);\n      if (storedUser) setUser(storedUser);\n    } catch {\n      setToken(null);\n      setUser(null);\n    }\n  }, []);\n\n  // Keep localStorage in sync\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (token) {\n      localStorage.setItem(\"token\", JSON.stringify(token));\n    } else {\n      localStorage.removeItem(\"token\");\n    }\n\n    if (user) {\n      localStorage.setItem(\"user\", JSON.stringify(user));\n    } else {\n      localStorage.removeItem(\"user\");\n    }\n  }, [token, user]);\n\n  return (\n    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, setUser, token, setToken }}>\n      {children}\n    </AuthContext.Provider>\n  );\n};\n\n// Custom hook for consuming AuthContext safely\nconst useAuth = () => {\n  const context = useContext(AuthContext);\n  if (!context) {\n    throw new Error(\"useAuth must be used within an AuthProvider\");\n  }\n  return context;\n};\n\nexport { AuthContext, AuthProvider, useAuth };"}]}

ASSISTANT:
{"updates":[{"pattern":".*","multiple":false,"replacement":"// src/context/AuthContext.jsx\nimport { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from \"react\";\n\nconst AuthContext = createContext();\n\nconst AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {\n  const [user, setUser] = useState(null);\n  const [token, setToken] = useState(null);\n\n  // Utility function to safely parse JSON\n  const safeParse = (value, fallback = null) => {\n    try {\n      return value ? JSON.parse(value) : fallback;\n    } catch {\n      return fallback;\n    }\n  };\n\n  // Load token and user from localStorage on mount\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const storedToken = safeParse(localStorage.getItem(\"token\"));\n    const storedUser = safeParse(localStorage.getItem(\"user\"));\n\n    if (storedToken) setToken(storedToken);\n    if (storedUser) setUser(storedUser);\n  }, []);\n\n  // Sync changes back to localStorage\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (token) {\n      localStorage.setItem(\"token\", JSON.stringify(token));\n    } else {\n      localStorage.removeItem(\"token\");\n    }\n\n    if (user) {\n      localStorage.setItem(\"user\", JSON.stringify(user));\n    } else {\n      localStorage.removeItem(\"user\");\n    }\n  }, [token, user]);\n\n  return (\n    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, setUser, token, setToken }}>\n      {children}\n    </AuthContext.Provider>\n  );\n};\n\n// Custom hook for consuming AuthContext safely\nconst useAuth = () => {\n  const context = useContext(AuthContext);\n  if (!context) {\n    throw new Error(\"useAuth must be used within an AuthProvider\");\n  }\n  const { user = null, setUser, token = null, setToken } = context;\n  return { user, setUser, token, setToken };\n};\n\nexport { AuthContext, AuthProvider, useAuth };"}]}

### **src/utils/api.js**
```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

// Add token interceptor
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = localStorage.getItem("token")
    ? JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token"))
    : null;
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named API functions
const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/register", userData);
  return res.data;
};

const login = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return res.data;
};

const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get("/projects");
  return res.data;
};

const createProject = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post("/projects", data);
  return res.data;
};

export { registerUser, login, getProjects, createProject };
```

---

### **src/components/Login.jsx**
```javascript
// src/components/Login.jsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { login } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

export default function Login() {
  const { setUser } = useAuth();
  const [email, setEmail] = useState("");
  const [password, setPassword] = useState("");

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await login({ email, password });
      localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(data.token));
      setUser(data.user);
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("Login failed:", err);
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="flex items-center justify-center min-h-screen bg-gray-100">
      <form
        onSubmit={handleSubmit}
        className="bg-white p-6 rounded-2xl shadow-md w-80"
      >
        <h2 className="text-xl font-bold mb-4 text-center">Login</h2>
        <input
          type="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          className="w-full p-2 mb-3 border rounded-lg"
          value={email}
          onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
          required
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          className="w-full p-2 mb-4 border rounded-lg"
          value={password}
          onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
          required
        />
        <button
          type="submit"
          className="w-full bg-blue-600 text-white p-2 rounded-lg hover:bg-blue-700 transition"
        >
          Login
        </button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
}
```

---

### **src/components/Dashboard.jsx**
```javascript
// src/components/Dashboard.jsx
import { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { getProjects } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";
import AddProjectForm from "./AddProjectForm";

export default function Dashboard() {
  const { user } = useAuth();
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const fetchProjects = async () => {
      try {
        const data = await getProjects();
        setProjects(data);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error("Failed to fetch projects:", err);
      }
    };
    fetchProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="p-6 bg-gray-100 min-h-screen">
      <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold mb-6">
        Welcome, {user?.name || "User"}
      </h1>
      <AddProjectForm onProjectAdded={(p) => setProjects([...projects, p])} />
      <div className="mt-6 grid gap-4 grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3">
        {projects.map((project) => (
          <div
            key={project._id}
            className="bg-white p-4 rounded-xl shadow-md border"
          >
            <h2 className="font-semibold text-lg">{project.name}</h2>
            <p className="text-gray-600">{project.description}</p>
          </div>
        ))}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

---

### **src/components/AddProjectForm.jsx**
```javascript
// src/components/AddProjectForm.jsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { createProject } from "../utils/api";

export default function AddProjectForm({ onProjectAdded }) {
  const [name, setName] = useState("");
  const [description, setDescription] = useState("");

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const newProject = await createProject({ name, description });
      onProjectAdded(newProject);
      setName("");
      setDescription("");
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("Failed to create project:", err);
    }
  };

  return (
    <form
      onSubmit={handleSubmit}
      className="bg-white p-4 rounded-xl shadow-md border w-full max-w-md"
    >
      <h2 className="text-lg font-semibold mb-4">Add Project</h2>
      <input
        type="text"
        placeholder="Project Name"
        className="w-full p-2 mb-3 border rounded-lg"
        value={name}
        onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)}
        required
      />
      <textarea
        placeholder="Project Description"
        className="w-full p-2 mb-3 border rounded-lg"
        value={description}
        onChange={(e) => setDescription(e.target.value)}
      />
      <button
        type="submit"
        className="w-full bg-green-600 text-white p-2 rounded-lg hover:bg-green-700 transition"
      >
        Create Project
      </button>
    </form>
  );
}
```

{"updates":[{"pattern":".*","multiple":false,"replacement":"// src/context/AuthContext.js\nimport { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from \"react\";\n\n// Create AuthContext\nconst AuthContext = createContext(null);\n\n// Helper function to safely parse JSON\nconst safeParse = (key, fallback) => {\n  try {\n    const value = localStorage.getItem(key);\n    return value ? JSON.parse(value) : fallback;\n  } catch (e) {\n    return fallback;\n  }\n};\n\n// AuthProvider component\nexport const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {\n  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse(\"user\", null));\n  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => safeParse(\"token\", null));\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (user) {\n      localStorage.setItem(\"user\", JSON.stringify(user));\n    } else {\n      localStorage.removeItem(\"user\");\n    }\n  }, [user]);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (token) {\n      localStorage.setItem(\"token\", JSON.stringify(token));\n    } else {\n      localStorage.removeItem(\"token\");\n    }\n  }, [token]);\n\n  const login = (userData, authToken) => {\n    setUser(userData);\n    setToken(authToken);\n  };\n\n  const logout = () => {\n    setUser(null);\n    setToken(null);\n    localStorage.removeItem(\"user\");\n    localStorage.removeItem(\"token\");\n  };\n\n  return (\n    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>\n      {children}\n    </AuthContext.Provider>\n  );\n};\n\n// Custom hook\nexport const useAuth = () => {\n  return useContext(AuthContext);\n};"}]}

JSON.parse(value) : fallback;\n    } catch (e) {\n      return fallback;\n    }\n  };\n\n  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse(\"user\", null));\n  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => safeParse(\"token\", null));\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (user) localStorage.setItem(\"user\", JSON.stringify(user));\n    else localStorage.removeItem(\"user\");\n  }, [user]);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (token) localStorage.setItem(\"token\", JSON.stringify(token));\n    else localStorage.removeItem(\"token\");\n  }, [token]);\n\n  const login = (userData, authToken) => {\n    setUser(userData);\n    setToken(authToken);\n  };\n\n  const logout = () => {\n    setUser(null);\n    setToken(null);\n    localStorage.removeItem(\"user\");\n    localStorage.removeItem(\"token\");\n  };\n\n  return (\n    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>\n      {children}\n    </AuthContext.Provider>\n  );\n};\n\nexport const useAuth = () => {\n  const context = useContext(AuthContext);\n  if (!context) {\n    throw new Error(\"useAuth must be used within an AuthProvider\");\n  }\n  return context;\n};"}]}

{"updates":[{"pattern":".*","multiple":false,"replacement":"// src/context/AuthContext.js\nimport { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from \"react\";\n\nconst AuthContext = createContext();\n\nexport const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {\n  const [user, setUser] = useState(null);\n  const [token, setToken] = useState(null);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    try {\n      const storedUser = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(\"user\"));\n      const storedToken = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(\"token\"));\n      if (storedUser) setUser(storedUser);\n      if (storedToken) setToken(storedToken);\n    } catch (e) {\n      setUser(null);\n      setToken(null);\n    }\n  }, []);\n\n  const login = (userData, tokenData) => {\n    setUser(userData);\n    setToken(tokenData);\n    localStorage.setItem(\"user\", JSON.stringify(userData));\n    localStorage.setItem(\"token\", JSON.stringify(tokenData));\n  };\n\n  const logout = () => {\n    setUser(null);\n    setToken(null);\n    localStorage.removeItem(\"user\");\n    localStorage.removeItem(\"token\");\n  };\n\n  return (\n    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>\n      {children}\n    </AuthContext.Provider>\n  );\n};\n\nexport const useAuth = () => {\n  return useContext(AuthContext);\n};"}]}

{"updates":[{"pattern":".*","multiple":false,"replacement":"// src/context/AuthContext.js\nimport { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from \"react\";\n\nconst AuthContext = createContext();\n\n// Safe localStorage parser\nconst safeParse = (key, fallback) => {\n  try {\n    const item = localStorage.getItem(key);\n    return item ? JSON.parse(item) : fallback;\n  } catch {\n    return fallback;\n  }\n};\n\nexport const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {\n  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse(\"user\", null));\n  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => safeParse(\"token\", null));\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (user) localStorage.setItem(\"user\", JSON.stringify(user));\n    else localStorage.removeItem(\"user\");\n  }, [user]);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (token) localStorage.setItem(\"token\", JSON.stringify(token));\n    else localStorage.removeItem(\"token\");\n  }, [token]);\n\n  const login = (userData, tokenData) => {\n    setUser(userData);\n    setToken(tokenData);\n  };\n\n  const logout = () => {\n    setUser(null);\n    setToken(null);\n  };\n\n  return (\n    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>\n      {children}\n    </AuthContext.Provider>\n  );\n};\n\nexport const useAuth = () => {\n  return useContext(AuthContext);\n};"}]}

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the consolidated fix package covering `AuthContext`, `api.js`, and consistent styling for `Login` and `Dashboard`:  

---

### `src/context/AuthContext.js`
```javascript
import React, { createContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

export const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    const storedUser = (() => {
      try {
        return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("user"));
      } catch {
        return null;
      }
    })();
    if (storedUser) setUser(storedUser);
  }, []);

  const login = (userData, token) => {
    localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(userData));
    localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(token));
    setUser(userData);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    localStorage.removeItem("user");
    localStorage.removeItem("token");
    setUser(null);
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};
```

---

### `src/utils/api.js`
```javascript
import axios from "axios";

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = (() => {
    try {
      return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token")) || null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  })();
  if (token) config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  return config;
});

export const login = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return res.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get("/projects");
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post("/projects", data);
  return res.data;
};

export default api;
```

---

### `src/components/Login.jsx`
```javascript
import React, { useState, useContext } from "react";
import { AuthContext } from "../context/AuthContext";
import { login as loginUser } from "../utils/api";

const Login = () => {
  const { login } = useContext(AuthContext);
  const [email, setEmail] = useState("");
  const [password, setPassword] = useState("");
  const [error, setError] = useState(null);

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await loginUser({ email, password });
      login(data.user, data.token);
    } catch {
      setError("Invalid credentials");
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="flex items-center justify-center min-h-screen bg-gray-100">
      <form
        onSubmit={handleSubmit}
        className="bg-white shadow-lg rounded-2xl p-8 w-96"
      >
        <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold mb-6 text-center">Login</h2>
        <input
          type="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          value={email}
          onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
          className="w-full p-2 mb-4 border rounded"
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          value={password}
          onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
          className="w-full p-2 mb-4 border rounded"
        />
        <button
          type="submit"
          className="w-full bg-blue-600 text-white py-2 rounded hover:bg-blue-700"
        >
          Login
        </button>
        {error && <p className="text-red-500 text-sm mt-4">{error}</p>}
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

---

### `src/components/Dashboard.jsx`
```javascript
import React, { useEffect, useState, useContext } from "react";
import { getProjects } from "../utils/api";
import { AuthContext } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Dashboard = () => {
  const { user, logout } = useContext(AuthContext);
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const fetchProjects = async () => {
      try {
        const data = await getProjects();
        setProjects(data);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error("Failed to fetch projects", err);
      }
    };
    fetchProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-gray-50 p-8">
      <div className="flex justify-between items-center mb-6">
        <h2 className="text-3xl font-bold">Dashboard</h2>
        <div>
          <span className="mr-4 font-medium">{user?.email}</span>
          <button
            onClick={logout}
            className="bg-red-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded hover:bg-red-600"
          >
            Logout
          </button>
        </div>
      </div>
      <ul className="space-y-2">
        {projects.map((project) => (
          <li
            key={project._id}
            className="bg-white shadow rounded p-4 hover:bg-gray-100"
          >
            {project.name}
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Dashboard;
```

- Imports point to the corrected `../utils/api` and `../context/AuthContext`.

USER:
Refactor the API helper module to export named functions for login, getProjects, createProject, and default axios instance with baseURL and token interceptor.

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

// Interceptor to attach Authorization header if token exists
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = (() => {
    try {
      return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token")) || null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  })();
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named exports for API calls
export const login = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return res.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get("/projects");
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post("/projects", data);
  return res.data;
};

// Default export for configured axios instance
export default api;
```

```javascript
        onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)}
        className="flex-1 p-2 border rounded"

        type="submit"
        className="bg-green-600 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded hover:bg-green-700"

        Add
      </button>
    </form>
  );
};
```  

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.js
import React, { createContext, useState, useEffect, useContext } from "react";

export const AuthContext = createContext();

const safeParse = (key, fallback = null) => {
  try {
    const raw = localStorage.getItem(key);
    return raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : fallback;
  } catch {
    return fallback;
  }
};

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse("user"));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => safeParse("token"));

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("user");
    }
  }, [user]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(token));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("token");
    }
  }, [token]);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

// Custom hook for consuming auth context
export const useAuth = () => {
  return useContext(AuthContext);
};
```  

This refactor ensures `AuthContext` and `useAuth` safely parse `localStorage` values with fallbacks, preventing crashes from malformed or missing JSON.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
Here’s the corrected set of modules with fixes to **AuthContext exports/imports**, **API utility exports**, **Authorization headers**, and consistent **styling across Login and Dashboard**.  

```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

// Attach Authorization header
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = (() => {
    try {
      return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token")) || null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  })();
  if (token) config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  return config;
});

// API calls
export const registerUser = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/register", data);
  return res.data;
};

export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return res.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get("/projects");
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post("/projects", data);
  return res.data;
};
```

```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.js
import React, { createContext, useState, useEffect, useContext } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext();

const safeParse = (key, fallback = null) => {
  try {
    const raw = localStorage.getItem(key);
    return raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : fallback;
  } catch {
    return fallback;
  }
};

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse("user"));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => safeParse("token"));

  useEffect(() => {
    user
      ? localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user))
      : localStorage.removeItem("user");
  }, [user]);

  useEffect(() => {
    token
      ? localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(token))
      : localStorage.removeItem("token");
  }, [token]);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => useContext(AuthContext);
```

```javascript
// src/components/Login.jsx
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { loginUser } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Login = () => {
  const { login } = useAuth();
  const [email, setEmail] = useState("");
  const [password, setPassword] = useState("");
  const [error, setError] = useState(null);

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await loginUser({ email, password });
      login(data.user, data.token);
    } catch {
      setError("Invalid credentials. Try again.");
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="flex items-center justify-center min-h-screen bg-gray-100">
      <form
        onSubmit={handleSubmit}
        className="bg-white shadow-lg rounded-2xl p-8 w-96"
      >
        <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold mb-6 text-center text-gray-800">
          Login
        </h2>
        <input
          type="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          value={email}
          onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
          className="w-full p-3 mb-4 border rounded focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-400"
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          value={password}
          onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
          className="w-full p-3 mb-6 border rounded focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-400"
        />
        <button
          type="submit"
          className="w-full bg-blue-600 text-white py-2 rounded-lg hover:bg-blue-700 transition"
        >
          Login
        </button>
        {error && <p className="text-red-500 text-sm mt-4">{error}</p>}
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

```javascript
// src/components/Dashboard.jsx
import React, { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { getProjects } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";
import AddProjectForm from "./AddProjectForm";

const Dashboard = () => {
  const { user, logout } = useAuth();
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const fetchProjects = async () => {
      try {
        const data = await getProjects();
        setProjects(data);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error("Failed to fetch projects", err);
      }
    };
    fetchProjects();
  }, []);

  const handleProjectAdded = (project) =>
    setProjects((prev) => [...prev, project]);

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-gray-50 p-8">
      <header className="flex justify-between items-center mb-8">
        <h2 className="text-3xl font-bold text-gray-800">Dashboard</h2>
        <div className="flex items-center gap-4">
          <span className="font-medium text-gray-600">{user?.email}</span>
          <button
            onClick={logout}
            className="bg-red-500 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded-lg hover:bg-red-600 transition"
          >
            Logout
          </button>
        </div>
      </header>

      <section>
        <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-4">Projects</h3>
        <ul className="space-y-2">
          {projects.map((project) => (
            <li
              key={project._id}
              className="bg-white shadow rounded-lg p-4 hover:bg-gray-100 transition"
            >
              {project.name}
            </li>
          ))}
        </ul>
        <AddProjectForm onProjectAdded={handleProjectAdded} />
      </section>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Dashboard;
```

```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.js
import React, { createContext, useState, useEffect, useContext } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext();

const safeParse = (key, fallback = null) => {
  try {
    const raw = localStorage.getItem(key);
    return raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : fallback;
  } catch {
    return fallback;
  }
};

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse("user"));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => safeParse("token"));

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("user");
    }
  }, [user]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(token));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("token");
    }
  }, [token]);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => useContext(AuthContext);
```

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

// Attach Authorization header with token from localStorage
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  try {
    const token = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token")) || null;
    if (token) config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  } catch {
    // ignore parsing errors
  }
  return config;
});

// Named API functions
export const login = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return res.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get("/projects");
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post("/projects", data);
  return res.data;
};

// Export default axios instance
export default api;
```

```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

// Attach Authorization header with token from localStorage
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  try {
    const token = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token")) || null;
    if (token) config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  } catch {
    // ignore parsing errors
  }
  return config;
});

// Named API functions
export const registerUser = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/register", data);
  return res.data;
};

export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return res.data;

  return res.data;
};
```

```javascript
// src/components/AddProjectForm.jsx
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { createProject } from "../utils/api";

const AddProjectForm = ({ onProjectAdded }) => {
  const [name, setName] = useState("");

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    if (!name.trim()) return;
    try {
      const project = await createProject({ name });
      onProjectAdded(project);
      setName("");
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("Failed to create project", err);
    }
  };

  return (
    <form
      onSubmit={handleSubmit}
      className="mt-6 bg-white shadow-md rounded-lg p-4 flex items-center gap-2"
    >
      <input
        type="text"
        placeholder="New Project"
        value={name}
        onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)}
        className="flex-1 p-2 border rounded focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-400"
      />
      <button
        type="submit"
        className="bg-blue-600 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded-lg hover:bg-blue-700 transition"
      >
        Add
      </button>
    </form>
  );
};

export default AddProjectForm;
```  

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.jsx
import { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext(null);

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  // Safely parse localStorage with fallback defaults
  const getStoredValue = (key, fallback) => {
    try {
      const raw = localStorage.getItem(key);
      return raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : fallback;
    } catch {
      return fallback;
    }
  };

  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => getStoredValue("user", null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => getStoredValue("token", null));

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("user");
    }
  }, [user]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(token));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("token");
    }
  }, [token]);

  const login = (userData, tokenData) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(tokenData);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
    localStorage.removeItem("user");
    localStorage.removeItem("token");
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

// Hook with fallback safety
export const useAuth = () => {
  const context = useContext(AuthContext);
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useAuth must be used within an AuthProvider");
  }
  return context;
};
```  

This update ensures:  
- All `localStorage` values are parsed safely with try/catch.  
- Defaults (`null`) are used if parsing fails.  
- `useAuth` throws only if called outside `AuthProvider`, not on bad storage data.

USER:
Refactor the API utility module to use named exports for login, registerUser, getProjects, createProject, attach auth token via interceptor, and remove default export.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Axios instance with baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

// Interceptor to attach Authorization header
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  try {
    const token = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token")) || null;
    if (token) {
      config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
    }
  } catch {
    /* ignore parse errors */
  }
  return config;
});

// Named API functions
export const login = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return res.data;
};

export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/register", userData);
  return res.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get("/projects");
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post("/projects", data);
  return res.data;
};
```  

This refactor:  
- Uses **named exports only** (`login`, `registerUser`, `getProjects`, `createProject`).  
- Removes the default export.  
- Attaches the token automatically in every request through the interceptor.  
- Cleans up duplicate `/api` prefixes by using correct endpoint paths.  

I’ll update the `chatGPT_Todo.txt` task list to reflect this change if you want.

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.jsx
import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext(null);

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  // Safe parse helper
  const safeParse = (key, fallback = null) => {
    try {
      const value = localStorage.getItem(key);
      return value ? JSON.parse(value) : fallback;
    } catch {
      return fallback;
    }
  };

  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse("user", null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => safeParse("token", null));

  useEffect(() => {
    if (user) {
      localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(user));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("user");
    }
  }, [user]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (token) {
      localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(token));
    } else {
      localStorage.removeItem("token");
    }
  }, [token]);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => {
  const context = useContext(AuthContext);
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useAuth must be used within an AuthProvider");
  }
  return context;
};
```  

This update ensures:  
- `localStorage` values are safely parsed with a helper `safeParse`.  
- Fallback defaults (`null`) are used if parsing fails or keys are missing.  
- `useAuth` continues to provide access to `user`, `token`, `login`, and `logout`.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.jsx
import { createContext, useContext, useState } from "react";

// Utility: safe JSON parse with fallback
const safeParse = (key, fallback) => {
  try {
    const value = localStorage.getItem(key);
    return value ? JSON.parse(value) : fallback;
  } catch {
    return fallback;
  }
};

const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse("user", null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => safeParse("token", null));

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
    localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(userData));
    localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(authToken));
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
    localStorage.removeItem("user");
    localStorage.removeItem("token");
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

// Updated hook
export const useAuth = () => {
  return useContext(AuthContext);
};
```  

This refactor:  
- Adds a `safeParse` utility for localStorage reads with fallback values.  
- Initializes `user` and `token` using safe parsing.  
- Keeps `login` and `logout` methods updating both state and localStorage.  
- Leaves `useAuth` as a clean, direct hook.

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.jsx
import { createContext, useContext, useState } from "react";

// Utility function: safely parse JSON from localStorage with fallback
const safeParse = (key, fallback) => {
  try {
    const item = localStorage.getItem(key);
    return item ? JSON.parse(item) : fallback;
  } catch {
    return fallback;
  }
};

const AuthContext = createContext(null);

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(() => safeParse("user", null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(() => safeParse("token", null));

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
    localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(userData));
    localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(authToken));
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
    localStorage.removeItem("user");
    localStorage.removeItem("token");
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

// Hook to access AuthContext
export const useAuth = () => useContext(AuthContext);
```

```javascript
    const token = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token")) || null;
    if (token) {
      config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
    }
  } catch {
    /* ignore invalid JSON */
  }
  return config;
});

// Named API functions
export const login = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return res.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get("/projects");
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post("/projects", data);
  return res.data;
};

// Export axios instance as default
export default api;
```  

This refactor exports:  
- `login`, `getProjects`, and `createProject` as **named functions**.  
- The configured `axios` instance (`api`) as the **default export**, with baseURL and token interceptor applied.

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Axios instance with correct baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000", // no /api prefix here
});

// Add Authorization token if available
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  try {
    const token = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token")) || null;
    if (token) {
      config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
    }
  } catch {
    /* ignore parse errors */
  }
  return config;
});

// API functions
export const login = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return res.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get("/projects");
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post("/projects", data);
  return res.data;
};

export default api;
```

```javascript
// src/pages/Login.jsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { useNavigate } from "react-router-dom";
import { login } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";

const Login = () => {
  const [form, setForm] = useState({ email: "", password: "" });
  const [error, setError] = useState("");
  const navigate = useNavigate();
  const { login: authLogin } = useAuth();

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    setForm({ ...form, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await login(form);
      authLogin(data.user, data.token);
      navigate("/dashboard");
    } catch {
      setError("Invalid email or password");
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="login-container">
      <h2>Login</h2>
      {error && <p className="error">{error}</p>}
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
        <input
          type="email"
          name="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          value={form.email}
          onChange={handleChange}
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          name="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          value={form.password}
          onChange={handleChange}
        />
        <button type="submit">Login</button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

```javascript
// src/pages/Dashboard.jsx
import { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { getProjects } from "../utils/api";
import AddProjectForm from "../components/AddProjectForm";

const Dashboard = () => {
  const [projects, setProjects] = useState([]);

  const fetchProjects = async () => {
    try {
      const data = await getProjects();
      setProjects(data);
    } catch {
      console.error("Failed to fetch projects");
    }
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="dashboard-container">
      <h2>Dashboard</h2>
      <AddProjectForm onProjectAdded={fetchProjects} />
      <ul>
        {projects.map((p) => (
          <li key={p._id}>{p.name}</li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Dashboard;
```

```javascript
// src/components/AddProjectForm.jsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { createProject } from "../utils/api";

const AddProjectForm = ({ onProjectAdded }) => {
  const [name, setName] = useState("");

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    if (!name.trim()) return;
    try {
      await createProject({ name });
      setName("");
      if (onProjectAdded) onProjectAdded();
    } catch {
      console.error("Failed to create project");
    }
  };

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="add-project-form">
      <input
        type="text"
        placeholder="New project name"
        value={name}
        onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)}
      />
      <button type="submit">Add Project</button>
    </form>
  );
};

export default AddProjectForm;
```  

All four files are now aligned to:  
- Use `http://localhost:5000` as the baseURL.  
- Remove any redundant `/api` prefix.  
- Use the centralized `api` instance with token interceptor.  
- Import functions (`login`, `getProjects`, `createProject`) cleanly from `utils/api`.

```javascript
// Attach Authorization token if available
```  

```javascript
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with correct baseURL
const api = axios.create({

// Attach Authorization token if available
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  try {
    const token = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token")) || null;
    if (token) {
      config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
    }
  } catch {
    /* ignore parse errors */
  }
  return config;
```  

ASSISTANT:
### Updated AuthContext (fix exports/imports)

```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.jsx
import { createContext, useContext, useState } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(null);
  const [token, setToken] = useState(localStorage.getItem("token") || null);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
    localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(authToken));
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
    localStorage.removeItem("token");
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => useContext(AuthContext);
```

```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",

api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  try {

    }
  } catch {
    /* ignore parse errors */
  }
```

### Restored Login.jsx (consistent style)

```javascript
// src/pages/Login.jsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { useNavigate } from "react-router-dom";
import { loginUser } from "../utils/api";
import { useAuth } from "../context/AuthContext";
import "./Login.css"; // consistent styling

const Login = () => {
  const [form, setForm] = useState({ email: "", password: "" });
  const [error, setError] = useState("");
  const navigate = useNavigate();
  const { login } = useAuth();

  const handleChange = (e) => {
    setForm({ ...form, [e.target.name]: e.target.value });
  };

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      const data = await loginUser(form);
      login(data.user, data.token);
      navigate("/dashboard");
    } catch {
      setError("Invalid email or password");
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="auth-container">
      <h2 className="auth-title">Login</h2>
      {error && <p className="auth-error">{error}</p>}
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="auth-form">
        <input
          type="email"
          name="email"
          placeholder="Email"
          value={form.email}
          onChange={handleChange}
          className="auth-input"
        />
        <input
          type="password"
          name="password"
          placeholder="Password"
          value={form.password}
          onChange={handleChange}
          className="auth-input"
        />
        <button type="submit" className="auth-button">Login</button>
      </form>
    </div>
  );
};

export default Login;
```

```javascript
      const data = await getProjects();
      setProjects(data);
    } catch {
      console.error("Failed to fetch projects");
    }
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchProjects();
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="dashboard-container">
      <h2 className="dashboard-title">Dashboard</h2>
      <AddProjectForm onProjectAdded={fetchProjects} />
      <ul className="dashboard-list">
        {projects.map((p) => (
          <li key={p._id} className="dashboard-item">
            {p.name}
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </div>
  );
};
```

```javascript
  const handleChange = (e) => {

      login(data.user, data.token);
      navigate("/dashboard");
    } catch {
      setError("Invalid email or password");
    }

      <h2 className="auth-title">Login</h2>

          type="email"

          value={form.email}
```

```javascript
// src/components/AddProjectForm.jsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { createProject } from "../utils/api";
import "./AddProjectForm.css";

const AddProjectForm = ({ onProjectAdded }) => {
  const [name, setName] = useState("");

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    if (!name.trim()) return;
    try {
      await createProject({ name });
      setName("");
      if (onProjectAdded) onProjectAdded();
    } catch {
      console.error("Failed to create project");
    }
  };

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="project-form">
      <input
        type="text"
        placeholder="Project Name"
        value={name}
        onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)}
        className="project-input"
      />
      <button type="submit" className="project-button">Add</button>
    </form>
  );
};

export default AddProjectForm;
```  

```javascript
const AuthContext = createContext();

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(null);
  const [token, setToken] = useState(localStorage.getItem("token") || null);

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
    localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(authToken));
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
    localStorage.removeItem("token");
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}

  );
```

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create axios instance with baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

// Attach Authorization token if available
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token") || "null");
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named API functions
export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/register", userData);
  return res.data;
};

export const login = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return res.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get("/projects");
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post("/projects", data);
  return res.data;
};
```

USER:
Update AuthContext to safely parse values from localStorage, using fallback defaults, and adjust useAuth hook accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/context/AuthContext.jsx
import { createContext, useContext, useState } from "react";

const AuthContext = createContext();

const safeParse = (key, fallback = null) => {
  try {
    const value = localStorage.getItem(key);
    return value ? JSON.parse(value) : fallback;
  } catch {
    return fallback;
  }
};

export const AuthProvider = ({ children }) => {
  const [user, setUser] = useState(safeParse("user", null));
  const [token, setToken] = useState(safeParse("token", null));

  const login = (userData, authToken) => {
    setUser(userData);
    setToken(authToken);
    localStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(userData));
    localStorage.setItem("token", JSON.stringify(authToken));
  };

  const logout = () => {
    setUser(null);
    setToken(null);
    localStorage.removeItem("user");
    localStorage.removeItem("token");
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, token, login, logout }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
};

export const useAuth = () => {
  return useContext(AuthContext);
};
```

   - `Login.jsx` should import `login` (not `loginUser`).

   - `AddProjectForm.jsx` should import `createProject`.  

4. **Ensure consistent styles**  
   - `Login.css` and `Dashboard.css` should both follow the same design system:  
     - **Containers**: centered, max-width 400px–600px.  
     - **Titles**: larger, bold, primary color.  
     - **Inputs**: full width, padding, border-radius.  
     - **Buttons**: consistent primary background, hover effect.

```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Axios instance
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

// Attach Authorization header if token exists
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token") || "null");
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named exports matching component imports
export const registerUser = async (userData) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/register", userData);
  return res.data;
};

export const loginUser = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return res.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get("/projects");
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post("/projects", data);
  return res.data;
};
```

ASSISTANT:
```javascript
// src/utils/api.js
import axios from "axios";

// Create default axios instance with baseURL
const api = axios.create({
  baseURL: "http://localhost:5000",
});

// Attach Authorization token interceptor
api.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  const token = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("token") || "null");
  if (token) {
    config.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  }
  return config;
});

// Named function exports
export const login = async (credentials) => {
  const res = await api.post("/auth/login", credentials);
  return res.data;
};

export const getProjects = async () => {
  const res = await api.get("/projects");
  return res.data;
};

export const createProject = async (data) => {
  const res = await api.post("/projects", data);
  return res.data;
};

// Export default axios instance
export default api;
```
